comment: resolves the coordinate field for an object by following selector-0x11 links, with arche fallback and kind-0x0100 match/mismatch blocks

name: vm_c2_descript_lookup
comment: C2 kind-0x0400 helper: opens descript.des and dispatches matching descriptor script

name: nav_actor_slot_update_loop
comment: walks six 0x18-byte navigation actor/object slots and dispatches via cs:0x06d4

name: nav_actor_subdispatch_call
comment: indirect call through cs:0x06d4 actor slot table

name: nav_actor_subdispatch_table
comment: six u16 near offsets: 07bc,06e0,095a,099e,0a1b,08a2

name: nav_actor_handler_1
comment: temporary label: cs:0x06d4 table entry 1

name: nav_actor_handler_0
comment: temporary label: cs:0x06d4 table entry 0

name: nav_actor_handler_5
comment: temporary label: cs:0x06d4 table entry 5

name: nav_actor_handler_2

name: nav_choice_dispatch
comment: navigation-choice hit-test and activation preamble before cs:0x0f29 table dispatch

name: nav_choice_subdispatch_call

name: nav_choice_subdispatch_table
comment: five u16 near offsets: 0f33,0f4c,0fdd,1068,108c

name: nav_choice_handler_0
comment: if phase bit set, defers C3 record link to named Honk object and clears phase

name: nav_choice_handler_1
comment: target-list choice handler: adjusts target records, waits interpolation, defers C3 target, reloads radio.snd

name: nav_choice_handler_2

name: nav_choice_handler_3

name: nav_choice_handler_4
comment: five-way handler: menu gate, tablo2 VOC toggle, left/right motion gates, sound gate

name: snd_son_name
comment: son.snd voice/SFX bank filename

name: snd_mus_name
comment: mus.snd music bank filename

name: descript_des_name
comment: descript.des filename opened by C2 helper 0x7409

name: script_basenames
comment: table of scriptN.cod/.bas/.var/.dic/.deb names (16-byte stride)

name: font_ascii_map
comment: dialogue font ASCII map (DS:0x7802)

name: font_advances
comment: dialogue font glyph advance widths

name: font_glyphs
comment: dialogue font 8-byte glyph bitmaps

name: resource_handle_resolve
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0x190: resolve resource handle AX->loaded segment. bx=AX<<3 (8-byte entries); if fs:[bx+2]&3 set, ds=fs:[bx]=resource segment, si=0, ax=1(loaded) else ax=0. Resource handle table = fs base, {seg u16, flags u16, ...} per 8-byte entry

name: resource_name_table_full
comment: FS:0x0c04 complete 16-byte resource-filename table indexed by resource ID: 0 fupcom.spr, 1 nosound.drv, 2-6 script1.{cod,bas,var,dic,deb}, 7 radio, 8-12 buffer, 13-14 bappel, 15 btv, 16 borxx, 17 bcarte, 18 bhyper, 19 bpol, 20 aphyper, 21 appol, 22-36 worlds (black..cyber), 37-41 script2 set, 42 dnsdb.drv, 43 corpo, 44 carte, 45 bigark, 46+ cyber2/3 + sub-levels + script3/4/5

name: resource_release
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0xf8: release resource handle AX if loaded. bx=AX<<3; if fs:[bx+2]&3 (loaded flag) set, call 0x529c (actual free). Resource table entry = 8 bytes {segment u16@+0, flags u16@+2 (bits0-1=loaded), ...}. Counterpart to resource_handle_resolve 0x5320

name: resource_free_inner
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0x10c: actual resource release (segment dealloc) called by resource_release once the loaded-flag check passes

name: resource_load_by_id
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x59b: load resource by ID AX. ds=fs; si=0x0c04+AX*16 (FS:0x0c04 name-table 16-byte entry = filename); call 0x28ca (lookup/already-loaded), lcall 0x4b9:0 (alloc slot), call 0x2abb (open+read file into segment). Returns ax=success. THE entry point for loading worlds/scripts: e.g. load venusia = resource_load_by_id(25). Called per-resource by vm_resource_profile_select 0x53da

name: resource_name_lookup
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x5ea: given DS:SI = FS:0x0c04 name-table entry, resolve/check the resource; returns ebp (0 = skip/already-resolved)

name: resource_file_load
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x7db: open the resource filename (via gs-relative path builder 0x2693) + read the file into the allocated resource segment; the loaded world/.ext data then lives at the resource segment (see resource_handle_resolve 0x5320)

name: resource_file_size
comment: loaded resource's file size (dword), read from the DOS FindFirst DTA at +0x1a during resource_file_load 0x2abb; also mirrored at GS:0x0A92. Bounds the read of the world/.ext data into its segment

name: resource_load_findfirst_size
comment: resource_file_load inner: int21h 0x2f00 (get DTA), 0x4e00 (FindFirst by built path) -> file size from DTA+0x1a into GS:0x0A8E/0A92, then int21h 0x3d00 (open) + read the bytes into the resource segment

name: entity_object_table
comment: 32-byte object/entity records indexed by object-id<<5 (gs-relative). Fields observed: +0x00 flags (|0x83 active/type bits, from resource [si]&4), +0x04 data offset + +0x06 data segment (far ptr into a loaded resource segment), +0x0c/+0x0e first two data words (NOT screen position - live gameplay bytes show a far-ptr shape e.g. 0x799a:0x0049; on-screen x/y is DERIVED at draw time from camera/viewport globals 0x277e/0x2780/0x2782/0x2aab/0x2aad + descriptor size, see entity_draw 0x9240), +0x14/+0x16 initial backup of +0xc/+0xe (reset-to values). Populated by entity_object_populate 0x40d0

name: entity_object_populate
comment: populate entity_object_table[AX] from resource handle DX: di=0x6212+AX<<5; lcall resource_handle_resolve 0x5320 (ds=resource seg,si); bound-check bp<[si+2]; flags=([si]&4)|0x83 -> +0x00; unpack packed dword ds:[bp<<2+si] into a far ptr (low nibble=extra si offset, >>4=segment delta added to ds) -> +0x04/+0x06; copy data words -> +0x0c/+0x0e, init +0x14/+0x16. First decode of the object-instance system

name: entity_object_populate_from_src
comment: variant of entity_object_populate: fills entity_object_table[AX] (si=0x6212+AX<<5) from a caller-supplied source ES:DI instead of a resolved resource. Identical record layout (+0x00 flags=([di]&4)|0x83, +0x04/+0x06 unpacked far ptr, +0x0c/+0x0e data words, +0x14/+0x16 init backups) - two independent routines agreeing CONFIRMS the 32-byte entity record structure

name: entity_get_flags
comment: AX=object id -> AX = flags word gs:[0x6212+id<<5] (entity record +0x00). Simple getter into entity_object_table

name: entity_flag_state_transition
comment: AX=object id: read flags gs:[0x6212+id<<5]; if active (bit7/0x80 set, js) AND bit0(0x01) set, clear bit0 and set bit1 (al=(al&0xfe)|2); store back. Per-object flag state machine - entity flags +0x00 bits: 0x80=active, 0x01/0x02 = state (0x83 init = active+state0+bit1). Transitions state0->state-advance

name: entity_flag_toggle_bit6
comment: AX=object id: read flags gs:[0x6212+id<<5]; if active(bit7/0x80), xor al,0x40 (toggle bit6) + state-advance (if bit0 set, set bit1); store back. Member of the entity flag-toggle family (0x41d1/0x4270/0x428c...) each toggling a distinct state bit gated on active

name: entity_flags_bitmap
comment: entity record +0x00 flags-word bit map (from the toggle-routine family): 0x80=active, 0x01/0x02=state pair (0x01->0x02 advance), 0x20=bit5 toggle-state, 0x40=bit6 toggle-state, 0x04 carried from source data. 0x83 init = active+state0+bit1

name: active_entity_workcopy
comment: 32-byte working copy of the currently-selected object (mirrors an entity_object_table record). entity_make_active 0x4316 copies entity[id] here for per-object processing

name: entity_make_active
comment: AX=object id: si=0x6212+id<<5; set flags bit1 (or [si],2); rep movsd cx=8 (=32 bytes) copy the entity record to active_entity_workcopy GS:0x0AF2. The rep movsd cx=8 RE-CONFIRMS the 32-byte entity-record size. Selects an object as the active one for processing

name: text_wrap_centered
comment: centered multi-line text layout: walk chars (max index [0x131c]); break a line at a space once length>=0x1c(28); per line store (char_count @[bp], centered_x=-((count<<2)-0xa0) @[bp+2]) into a break-table at bp=0xaf2, cx=line count. Produces centered wrapped text (menu/message display). NOTE bp=0xaf2 here is a shared scratch buffer - NOT the entity workcopy despite the shared address

name: entity_flag_set_if_field8_mismatch
comment: AX=object id, BX=param: bx=0x6212+id<<5; if flags al&0x81 (active+state0) and gs:[record+8] != param DX, set state bit1 (or al,2). Uses entity field +0x08 as a comparable id/group/target - if it mismatches the param, advance the object's state

name: entity_field_08
comment: entity record +0x08: a comparable 16-bit id/group/target field (compared against a param in entity_flag_set_if_field8_mismatch 0x420d; mismatch -> state advance). Adds to the record map: +0x00 flags, +0x04/+0x06 far ptr, +0x08 id/group, +0x0c/+0x0e data words, +0x14/+0x16 init backups

name: input_action_dispatch
comment: main-loop per-frame input dispatcher: lcall 0x1ce:0x39d (poll event -> ax); form action byte (al, |0x80 for the high variant); al = xlat via table cs:0x113e (input->action index); if index>=0, call cs:[index*2 + 0x123e] = the action handler. Routes raw input to command handlers

name: input_action_xlat_table
comment: cs:0x113e: 256-entry xlatb table mapping a raw input/event byte to an action index (negative = ignore). Feeds input_action_dispatch 0x210e

name: input_action_jump_table
comment: cs:0x123e: word jump table of action-handler cs-offsets (handler file = 0x600+entry), called by input_action_dispatch. First 16 handlers -> file 0x1f06,0x652,0x6c0,0xa0a0,0x153e,0x899,0x6b66,0xc8f,0x5819,0xd5f,0x255e,0x56cb,0x5d06,0xa656,0x2d51,0x7a8. The game's command/action handler set

name: cmd_handler_pc_speaker_beep
comment: input-action handler (jump-table entry): call 0x99f, then program the PC speaker via the PIT (out 0x43,0xb6 = ch2 square wave; out 0x42,0x9c/0x2e = divisor 0x2e9c ~100Hz), lcall 0x8b:0 (speaker gate/sound). The game uses PC-speaker beeps for some SFX, separate from the VOC/cpal audio path

name: cmd_handler_numeric_value
comment: input-action handler: ax=bx; eax<<=4 (value*16) -> [0xa46] dword; bound-check vs 0x78870; if under, si=0xe + call 0xd61. Processes a scaled numeric input (scroll/position/selection value)

name: state_word_array
comment: 0x1e-word (or 0x40-dword) runtime state array; init to 0xffffffff by vm_resource_profile_select 0x53f6; iterated by cmd handler 0x899 and others

name: input_action_jump_table_CORRECTION
comment: CORRECTION to the earlier 0x183e note: the '16/16 valid code offsets' claim only meant the entries point into the code REGION - NOT that all are real handlers. Verified valid handler starts: 0x652 (numeric), 0x6c0 (PC-speaker beep), 0x899 (state array). But entries read as 0x153e and 0xc8f disassemble MID-INSTRUCTION, so the true handler count is < 16 and bounded by the xlat table's (0x113e) actual index range, not by 16. The dispatch MECHANISM (0x210e poll->xlat->call) is verified; the exact handler set needs the xlat-table index range to bound it. Do not treat all 16 raw entries as handlers

name: input_action_xlat_range_measured
comment: MEASURED bound (follow-up to the jump-table correction): the xlat table cs:0x113e has 159 non-negative entries mapping input/event bytes to action indices, MAX index = 125. So input_action_jump_table cs:0x123e has ~126 entries (index 0..125) = the game's full keyboard/event command set - FAR more than the 16 first sampled. ~93/126 pass a strict prologue check (undercount; valid handlers can start with sti/lea/shl/rep/etc not in the whitelist). The action set is large; individual handler identification is per-slot work

name: input_dispatch_handler_table_UNRESOLVED
comment: HONEST RETRACTION (2nd correction): sampling more jump-table entries (0x153e,0xc8f,0x5819,0x255e,0x5d06) shows MOST disassemble mid-instruction under the flat file=0x600+cs_offset mapping. So neither '16' nor the 'measured ~126' handler count is reliable, and the flat near-handler mapping is likely WRONG (the dispatcher's real CS base and/or near-vs-far call semantics are unverified). VERIFIED: only the dispatch MECHANISM at 0x210e (poll 0x1ce:0x39d -> xlatb via 0x113e -> call cs:[idx*2+0x123e]). NOT verified: the handler table's base/size/entries. The 3 'clean' handlers (0x652/0x6c0/0x899) may be coincidental alignment. Resolving needs the dispatcher's actual runtime CS + a jump-table entry whose target is cross-checked as a real routine entry

name: main_loop_calls_input_dispatch_VERIFIED
comment: VERIFIED: the main loop near-calls the input dispatcher - bytes E8 FD 10 at 0x100e, rel=0x10fd -> target 0x210e (confirms dispatcher identity + dis.py file-offset addressing). RESOLVED-AS-FAR-AS-STATIC-ALLOWS: the handler jump-table contents cannot be discriminated by prologue-checking - all 4 (table@0x123e/0x183e x handler+0/+0x600) combos give ~15/20 clean prologues, consistent with RANDOM alignment (most bytes decode as push/mov). Most-principled mapping is CS-base file 0x600 (first code segment) -> table@0x183e, handler=entry+0x600 - but individual handlers need a runtime trace or behavioral match to confirm, not static sampling. Dispatch mechanism = solid; handler enumeration = genuinely unresolved statically

name: input_jump_table_static_limit_CONFIRMED
comment: CONFIRMED static limit (final on this): a stronger reach-a-ret coherence test gives 126/126 = uninformative (any offset in dense code reaches ret/jmp within 40 insns), just as prologue-checking was ~random. So NO static test discriminates real handlers from mid-instruction offsets. The mapping (CS-base 0x600) is validated ONLY by semantically-meaningful handlers: 0x6c0 (PC-speaker beep - unambiguous), 0x652 (numeric), 0x899 (state array). Full handler enumeration REQUIRES runtime tracing (or per-input behavioral matching), not static analysis. Investigation closed here to avoid low-value churn; mechanism (0x210e) solid, 3 handlers real, rest deferred to a runtime method

name: main_loop_hud_refresh
comment: main-loop per-frame HUD refresh (gated on [0xadf]&1): program VGA sequencer out 0x3c4,ax=0x0f02 (map-mask = all 4 planes), les di,[0x521d] (screen buffer far ptr), clear a 20-byte x 14-row region at row-stride 0x50 (80 bytes/row), then lcall 0x299:0x498 (graphics draw) + lcall 0:0x5d7. CONFIRMS the game renders in mode-X (planar 320x200, 80 bytes/row/plane) via the VGA sequencer, not linear mode 13h. The engine's linear framebuffer produces identical visual output (mode-X is a perf/paging technique, not a visual difference)

name: screen_buffer_far_ptr
comment: far pointer to the mode-X screen/back buffer, used by the HUD refresh (0x1a93) and graphics routines; base for the 80-bytes/row planar layout

name: graphics_plot_modex
comment: SEG 0x299:0x498: mode-X pixel/span plot. Inputs bx=x, dx=y, al=colour. Bounds-check dx vs gs:[0x5239](min+0xa)/[0x523b](max). Address = screen_buffer_far_ptr(gs:0x521d) + y*80 (ax=y<<4 + dx=y<<6) + x/4 (bx>>2); plane = x&3 (cl=bl&3) selected via the VGA map-mask. Confirms mode-X layout: byte offset = y*80 + x/4, plane = x&3. The game's core rendering primitive (my engine's linear y*320+x framebuffer yields the same pixel)

name: screen_clip_bounds
comment: mode-X vertical clip bounds: gs:[0x5239] = height-related min (used as -0xa lower gate), gs:[0x523b] = max y; graphics_plot_modex rejects out-of-range dx

name: dirty_rect_blit
comment: SEG 0x299:0x210d: blit dirty rectangles from the LINEAR back-buffer (gs:0x5229) to the mode-X screen. Gated on dirty flag gs:[0x5231]&1. Per rect record es:[di]: +0x00=x(ax), +0x02=right/x2, +0x04=y(bx), +0x06=width(cx). Source offset = y*320+x (bx*256 via xchg + bx*64 via shl6 = y*320, +x) - the back buffer is LINEAR. Row-skip stride = 0x140-width. KEY: the game renders to a linear back-buffer then dirty-rect-blits to mode-X, so the engine's linear framebuffer matches the game's INTERNAL representation, not just address-equivalent

name: linear_back_buffer
comment: far pointer to the game's LINEAR (y*320+x) back-buffer/composition surface; dirty_rect_blit 0x509d copies rects from here to the mode-X VGA screen. Same layout as the engine's linear framebuffer

name: dirty_flag
comment: back-buffer dirty flag; bit0 gates dirty_rect_blit 0x509d

name: back_buffer_fill
comment: SEG 0x299:region: fill a horizontal band of the LINEAR back-buffer (gs:0x5229) with colour al. di = gs:[0x5239]*320 (top row: cx*256 via xchg + cx*64 via shl6); count = (gs:[0x523b]-gs:[0x5239])*80 dwords (= height*320 bytes); al replicated to eax (ah=al, shl16, ax=bx); rep stosd. Re-confirms the linear y*320 back-buffer layout + gs:0x5239/0x523b as the top/bottom band-row bounds. A screen/band clear before compositing

name: sprite_blit_rle
comment: RLE sprite decode+composite into the LINEAR back-buffer (les di,gs:0x5229). ebp=0xfa00 (64000=320x200 pixel budget). Per token: lodsb control; if negative -> run length cl=(-al+1), sub ebp; lodsb pixel: if 0 -> di+=cx (TRANSPARENT skip) else rep stosb (fill run with pixel); if control>=0 -> literal run (copy cl bytes). So SPR frames are RLE with transparency = index 0, composited into the linear surface (gated on gs:[0x5b57]&1). Matches the engine's SpriteFrameImage transparent-0 model

name: main_loop_busy_gate
comment: main-loop idle gate: OR 10 subsystem-active flags ([0x67ac] presentation, [0x24f3],[0x2751],[0x67b0],[0x5e64],[0x2565],[0x2736],[0x2737],[0x27da],[0x2792]); if ANY set, skip the pending-profile dispatch (defer scene load until all idle). Coordinates when a new script profile (dialogue scene / game state) may load

name: main_loop_is_presentation_coordinator
comment: CLARIFICATION: the top-level main loop 0x0FFB is the PRESENTATION/MENU coordinator - input dispatch (0x210e), HUD refresh (0x1a93), per-frame update (0x1fbc, lcall 0x4da:0x204), busy-gate (0x109c), then pending-profile dispatch (0x10c5 -> vm_resource_profile_select). It does NOT run the object-simulation; the entity/object sim + combat run inside game states loaded as script profiles, reached via this loop's profile dispatch - which is why the entity accessors (0x6212 cluster) have no iterator in the main loop

name: vm_run_wrapper_is_the_per_frame_gameplay
comment: ARCHITECTURE: the main loop's per-frame update (lcall 0x4da:0x204, gated on [0x27e0]&1) IS vm_run_wrapper 0x55a4 - it resolves the 5 script-profile resources (DS:0x6712->DS:0x671c far ptrs via 0x55d9) then enters vm_exec_loop, gated on VM-active [0x67a8]&1. KEY: gameplay = VM SCRIPT EXECUTION. The object-simulation, combat, and interactions are script opcodes the VM runs each frame over the loaded profile (COD/BAS/VAR/DIC) - NOT a separate native loop. Unifies the decoded subsystems: main loop -> vm_run_wrapper -> vm_exec_loop -> opcodes (A6 text/C1 ship-3d/C4 presentation/D2 profile) -> entity table (0x6212) + render path. The entity accessors are called from VM opcode handlers

name: vm_exec_loop_dispatch
comment: VM opcode fetch+dispatch: lds si,gs:0x671c (COD script far ptr, or gs:0x677a if resumed); lodsb al = opcode; if 0xff -> end; index bl=al-0xA0 (opcodes are 0xA0-based), bx=index*2; call gs:[bx + 0x6eb0] = opcode handler. Handler table at GS:0x6eb0 (runtime, populated at init). After: repeat/loop via gs:[0x67ab]/[0x67b4] + 0x62b6. A6=idx6, C1=idx0x21, C4=idx0x24, D2=idx0x32

name: vm_opcode_handler_table
comment: runtime VM opcode-handler jump table, indexed by (opcode-0xA0)*2 near-offset; populated at VM init. Enumerating it = the full VM opcode set / script command language

name: vm_opcode_table_enumeration_LIMIT
comment: STATIC LIMIT (same class as the input jump-table): the VM opcode-handler table's CONTENTS couldn't be enumerated statically. The A6-handler cs-offset (file0x660c-0x600=0x600c) appears nowhere word-aligned, and no contiguous table has A6@idx6 + D2@idx0x32 - so either the VM-code CS base != 0x600 (handlers in a non-base segment, offsets relative to a different base) or the table is populated by a non-obvious init. VERIFIED: the dispatch MECHANISM (vm_exec_loop_dispatch 0x5613: opcode-0xA0 -> call gs:[idx*2+0x6eb0]) + 4 known opcodes (A6/C1/C4/D2 already decoded in vm.rs). NOT resolved: the full opcode enumeration - needs the VM-code segment base or a runtime trace. Deferred rather than guessed, like the input jump table

name: vm_opcode_handler_table_static
comment: the VM's STATIC opcode-handler table (copied to runtime GS:0x6eb0 at init). ENUMERATED: opcodes 0xA0.. , entry i = handler offset in VM code segment 0x4da (handler file = entry + 0x53a0). Validated vs A6(0x660c)/C1(0x6b4c)/C4(0x6c7e)/D2(0x64b8). ~50 opcodes, ~30 distinct handlers (many share defaults 0x6946/0x6863). The full script command set - see REVERSE.md VM opcode table. Resolved by cracking the VM-code segment base (0x4da->file 0x53a0), the limit deferred last turn

name: vm_op_a0_push
comment: VM opcode 0xA0 PUSH: set [0x67ad]=1; bp=stack_ptr gs:[0x6884]; stack_ptr+=2; lodsw operand -> [bp+0x6820]. Pushes the 16-bit operand onto the VM stack (gs:0x6820, ptr gs:0x6884)

name: vm_op_a1_pop
comment: VM opcode 0xA1 POP: set [0x67ad]=0; if stack_ptr gs:[0x6884]==2 (empty) noop else stack_ptr-=2. Pops the VM stack

name: vm_op_a2_cond_call
comment: VM opcode 0xA2: lodsw operand; lcall 0x1ce:0xb02 (condition/test); if ax!=0 call 0x6462. Conditional call/branch on the tested operand

name: vm_op_a3_block
comment: VM opcode 0xA3: conditional block - scans the script for the matching 0xA1 (POP) opcode, gated on gs:[0x67b2]&1 and comparing bytes to 0xa1. A structured block/if construct

name: vm_stack
comment: VM operand stack (16-bit entries); stack pointer at gs:0x6884 (starts at 2, +=2 push / -=2 pop). Used by opcodes 0xA0/0xA1

name: vm_branch
comment: VM branch/jump primitive: called by the conditional opcodes (0xA2/0xCE/0xD0) to take a script jump when their condition holds; consumes the branch-target operand from the script stream

name: vm_op_ce_cond_branch
comment: VM opcode 0xCE: if gs:[0x2793]&1 is CLEAR, call vm_branch 0x6462 (conditional jump on presentation-flag [0x2793] bit0)

name: vm_op_d0_cond_branch
comment: VM opcode 0xD0: if gs:[0x252a]&1 is CLEAR, call vm_branch 0x6462 (conditional jump on game-flag [0x252a] bit0)

name: vm_op_cf_clear_state
comment: VM opcode 0xCF: clear gs:[0x67b1]=0 and gs:[0x6764]=0 (reset resume/PC-related VM state)

name: vm_op_cc_set_record_byte
comment: VM opcode 0xCC: bp=0x6cde + (operand1-1)*16; [bp]=operand2. Writes a byte into a 16-byte-record table at gs:0x6cde indexed by (operand-1) - a set-property/set-variable op

name: vm_record_table_16b
comment: table of 16-byte records at gs:0x6cde, indexed by (operand-1); written by opcode 0xCC (set-property). Likely script variables / per-entity properties

name: vm_op_b7_record_op
comment: VM opcode 0xB7: les di,gs:[0x6724] (object/line-record state table); optional 0xA1(POP) skip; lodsw index/offset + lodsb value -> read/write a field in the state-table record. An object/line-record manipulation opcode

name: vm_op_b8_record_compare
comment: VM opcode 0xB8: les di,gs:[0x6724]; di += operand (record offset); lodsw compare-value bx; lodsw; if gs:[0x67ad]&1, cmp bx vs es:[di] (record field) - conditional on a state-table record field. Record-field compare/branch

name: vm_op_c2_record_call
comment: VM opcode 0xC2: les di,gs:[0x6724]; optional 0xA1 skip; lodsw bp; call 0x6034 - object/line-record op with a subcall. Manipulates the runtime state table

name: vm_op_c9_clear_record_field
comment: VM opcode 0xC9: les di,gs:[0x6724]; lodsw di = record offset; read cx=es:[di]; stosw 0 - clears a field in the object/line-record state table to 0. A reset-record-field op

name: vm_op_c5_state_record
comment: VM opcode 0xC5: object/line-record state op (les di,gs:0x6724 + 0xA1-skip prologue + lodsw operand). Part of the C-range state-table family; exact field operation NOT yet fully decoded (only the shared prologue confirmed)

name: vm_op_c6_state_record
comment: VM opcode 0xC6: object/line-record state op (same state-table + 0xA1-skip prologue as C5). C-range family member; exact operation not yet fully decoded

name: vm_op_c8_state_record
comment: VM opcode 0xC8: object/line-record state op (state-table + 0xA1-skip prologue). C-range family member; exact operation not yet fully decoded

name: vm_op_shared_state_gated
comment: VM opcode SHARED handler for 0xAD/0xAF/0xB2/0xB3/0xBA/0xBB/0xBC (7 opcodes -> same handler = same operation): les di,gs:[0x6724] state table; gated on gs:[0x67ad]&1; 0xA1-skip; lodsw bx record offset/value. A state-table record op these opcodes all perform identically (aliased/grouped opcodes). Exact field op partially decoded (prologue + record access confirmed)

name: vm_op_shared_state_marker
comment: VM opcode SHARED handler for 0xB1/0xB4/0xB5/0xB6/0xBE/0xBF/0xC0 (7 opcodes -> same handler): les di,gs:[0x6724]; bx=[si] offset; cx=es:[bx+di] read record field; lodsb ah + lodsb al operands; cmp al vs opcode markers 0xc0/0xc2. Reads a state-table record field + branches on following opcode markers. Grouped opcodes performing the same state-table op; exact op partially decoded

name: vm_op_a4_jump
comment: VM opcode 0xA4 JUMP: si=[si] (set script PC to the operand address); clear resume state gs:[0x67b1]=0, gs:[0x6764]=0. Unconditional jump

name: vm_op_a5_cond_state_array
comment: VM opcode 0xA5: lodsb index; bp=index*2; gated on gs:[0x67ad]&1; test word gs:[bp+0x6ade] (the state_word_array) - conditional branch on a state-array flag

name: vm_op_a7_set_if_presentation
comment: VM opcode 0xA7: lodsw operand; if gs:[0x67ac]&1 (presentation active) set gs:[0x6770]=operand. Conditional set-state

name: vm_op_a9_cond_jump
comment: VM opcode 0xA9: lodsb al; if bit0 CLEAR, si=[si] (jump to operand). Conditional jump on operand bit0

name: vm_op_aa_yield
comment: VM opcode 0xAA: set gs:[0x67b4]=1 - the yield flag the exec loop checks after each handler (0x562a); makes the VM break/yield the current frame. A yield/wait opcode

name: vm_op_a8_load_string
comment: VM opcode 0xA8: copy a null-terminated string operand from the script into a buffer at 0x2120 (lodsb/[bp]=al loop until 0), then re-reads it. A string set/load op (filename or text into the 0x2120 buffer)

name: vm_op_ab_poke_byte
comment: VM opcode 0xAB: lodsb al value; bx=[si] address; [bx]=al; si+=2. Writes a byte to an arbitrary DS address given by the operand - a set-variable/poke op

name: vm_op_ac_yield
comment: VM opcode 0xAC: set gs:[0x67b4]=1 (yield flag) - alias of 0xAA, yields/breaks the VM frame after this opcode

name: vm_op_c3_state_record
comment: VM opcode 0xC3: object/line-record state op (les di,gs:0x6724 + 0xA1-skip prologue). C-range state-table family; exact field op partial

name: vm_op_c7_state_record
comment: VM opcode 0xC7: object/line-record state op (state-table + 0xA1-skip prologue). C-range family; exact field op partial

name: vm_op_ca_compare_var
comment: VM opcode 0xCA: lodsw tag (dl), lodsw value; if tag==0xf1 (variable-ref marker) compare value vs gs:[0xaa6] and branch (jg). Conditional comparison of a script value/variable against game state [0xaa6]

name: vm_op_cb_compare_byte
comment: VM opcode 0xCB: lodsb tag, lodsw bx, lodsw; if tag==0xf1 compare bh vs gs:[0xaaa] and branch. Byte comparison of a script variable vs game state [0xaaa]. Companion to 0xCA

name: vm_op_shared_ae_b0_state
comment: VM opcodes 0xAE + 0xB0 (shared handler 0x6902): object/line-record state op (les di,gs:0x6724 + 0xA1-skip). State-table family; exact op partial

name: vm_op_cd_state_gated
comment: VM opcode 0xCD: state-table record op (les di,gs:0x6724, gated on gs:[0x67ad]&1, 0xA1-skip). State-table family; exact op partial

name: vm_compare_state_var
comment: game-state variables compared by opcodes 0xCA (word gs:[0xaa6]) / 0xCB (byte gs:[0xaaa]); the 0xf1 tag in the script marks a variable reference

name: vm_op_c5_record_match
comment: VM opcode 0xC5 FULL: les di,gs:0x6724; lodsw bp=record ptr, lodsw operand; gated on gs:[0x67ad]&1: cmp operand vs es:[bp+2] (record id/value field) AND cmp es:[bp] vs 0xC5 (record TYPE tag = the opcode value). So state-table records are TYPED - field +0 = the opcode/type that created them, +2 = id/value. Each C-range opcode (C5/C6/C7/C8) matches records of ITS OWN type (compares +0 to its own opcode). Record-match/conditional op

name: vm_state_record_layout
comment: object/line-record (gs:0x6724 table) field layout from the C-range decode: +0x00 = TYPE tag (the VM opcode that created the record, e.g. 0xC5/0xC6), +0x02 = id/value (matched against the opcode operand). Records are typed by their creating opcode; C-range opcodes match/operate on records of their own type

name: vm_op_d1_cond_branch
comment: VM opcode 0xD1: if gs:[0x274f]&1 is CLEAR, call vm_branch 0x6462. Conditional branch on game-flag [0x274f] bit0. Completes the conditional-branch opcode family: 0xCE([0x2793])/0xD0([0x252a])/0xD1([0x274f]) - each branches on a distinct game-state flag bit

name: vm_record_lookup_by_threshold
comment: record-lookup helper (called by opcode 0xC2): lds si,gs:[0x672c] (20-byte-record list); scan while ax > [si+0x10], si+=0x14; finds the record whose +0x10 threshold field >= ax. A keyed lookup into the gs:0x672c table

name: vm_lookup_table_20b
comment: table of 20-byte (0x14) records at gs:0x672c, keyed/searched by field +0x10 (vm_record_lookup_by_threshold 0x6034). A VM lookup/range table

name: vm_op_c6_record_match
comment: VM opcode 0xC6 FULL (confirmed same structure as C5): les di,gs:0x6724; lodsw bp record, lodsw operand; gated on [0x67ad]; cmp operand vs es:[bp+2] (id) AND cmp es:[bp] vs 0xC6 (self type-tag). Matches state-table records of type 0xC6. VERIFIES the C-range typed-record pattern (C5->0xc5, C6->0xc6, C8->0xc8)

name: vm_op_c8_record_match
comment: VM opcode 0xC8 FULL: identical structure, matches records tagged 0xC8 (cmp es:[bp] vs 0xc8). Confirms each C-range opcode matches records of its own type - the typed-record model is verified across C5/C6/C8, not hypothesized

name: vm_op_b8_record_readwrite
comment: VM opcode 0xB8 (shared B8/B9/BD): les di,gs:0x6724; di+=operand record offset; lodsw bx, lodsw ax (two values). If gs:[0x67ad]&1 (QUERY mode): cmp bx vs es:[di] AND ax vs es:[di+2]; both match -> continue, else vm_branch. If NOT query (SET mode): es:[di]=bx, es:[di+2]=ax (WRITE both). A dual-mode record read/write of a 2-word record field pair

name: vm_op_c7_record_match
comment: VM opcode 0xC7: matches state-table records tagged 0xC7 (cmp es:[bp] vs 0xc7). Confirms the typed-record pattern across C5/C6/C7/C8 - all four verified

name: vm_query_mode_flag
comment: KEY: the VM QUERY-vs-SET mode flag. Set by 0xA0 PUSH, cleared by 0xA1 POP. When SET, record opcodes (B8/C5-C8/0x6946/etc.) COMPARE their fields and branch (query/if); when CLEAR they WRITE (set). So 'A0 <record-queries> A1' is a conditional block; record ops outside it perform sets. Unifies the whole VM opcode model

name: vm_op_shared_record_wildcard
comment: VM shared handler for 0xAD/0xAF/0xB2/0xB3/0xBA/0xBB/0xBC (7 opcodes, same op): dual-mode record op on gs:0x6724[bx+di], gated on the query flag gs:[0x67ad]. QUERY mode: lodsw bx offset + lodsw value; WILDCARD - if value==gs:[0x674e] substitute 0xffff (match-any); cmp vs es:[bx+di], branch via 0x6462 on mismatch. SET mode: write path (0x6985). These 7 opcode values all execute this same generic record compare/write with wildcard - likely script-readability verbs mapping to one op

name: vm_wildcard_ref_value
comment: the VM wildcard sentinel: in the shared record handler 0x6946, a compare value equal to gs:[0x674e] is replaced by 0xffff (match-any/current). Enables 'any' matches in record queries

name: vm_state_operator_set
comment: COMPLETE operator byte set for the 0x6863 compound state opcodes (operator in ah, cx=state[bx+di], dx=operand). QUERY mode (gs:[0x67ad] set, compare -> branch on fail via 0x6462): 0xF0 != (setne), 0xF1 < (setl), 0xF2 > (setg), 0xF3 <= (setle), 0xF4 >= (setge), 0xF5 == (sete). SET mode (assign, write es:[bx+di]=cx at 0x68fd): 0xF5 SET (cx=dx), 0xF6 ADD (cx+=dx), 0xF7 SUB (cx-=dx). Resolves the dead_ends open sub-task: the 'plain set' operator = 0xF5 in set mode. So state ops = state[op1] OP op2 with OP in {ne,lt,gt,le,ge,eq}(query) / {set,add,sub}(assign)

name: gfx_scanline_advance
comment: rendering row helper (22 call sites - most-used unlabeled utility): dec row counter [bp-6]; if 0 return via 0xada9; else di=[bp-8]+0x140 (advance one scanline = 320 bytes in the linear back-buffer), store [bp-8]=di, cx=[bp-0xa]. The per-row advance used across the render/blit loops

name: back_buffer_copy_from
comment: blit helper (10 calls): les di,gs:[0x5229] (linear back-buffer dest); lds si,gs:[0xabc] (source surface); copies from the gs:0xabc source into the back buffer. A surface->backbuffer copy

name: presentation_line_helper
comment: presentation/dialogue helper (8 calls): gated on [0x2793]&8 (presentation-busy) and [bp]&4; processes a presentation line record. Part of the dialogue/subtitle presentation path

name: lookup_table_1fb5
comment: 4-byte-record table accessor (5 calls): bx=0x1fb5 + ax*4 (add bx,ax x4); return word [bx]. Indexes a table of 4-byte records at DS:0x1fb5 by AX, returning the first word. A data-structure lookup helper

name: screen_flags_init
comment: state-init helper (4 calls): [0x27d9]=0; [0x5b53]=1; [0x5b55]=1. Sets screen/presentation-ready flags - part of a mode/screen setup sequence

name: vm_segment_call_wrapper
comment: wrapper (4 calls): saves regs, lcall 0x4da:0x1c53 (into the VM code segment), post-processes with gs. A thunk into a VM-segment routine

name: table_1fb5_4byte_records
comment: table of 4-byte records at DS:0x1fb5, indexed by AX; accessed via lookup_table_1fb5 0x9f80

name: bcd_to_binary
comment: packed-BCD-to-binary byte helper (4 calls): bl=al&0xf (low nibble); al=(al>>4)*10 + bl. Converts a packed BCD byte (e.g. a CMOS RTC date/time field) to binary 0..99

name: page_flip
comment: page-flip / display-swap (4 calls): [0x5b55]=1; save [0x5221]; copy the linear back-buffer far ptr [0x5229] into the display far ptr [0x5221]; lcall 0x299:0xdeb (present) + call 0x98b9. Swaps the composed back buffer to the visible page

name: page_offset_helper
comment: screen page-offset helper (3 calls): ax=[0x5219]; if negative ax=0 else ax += 0x4000. Adds the VGA page offset (0x4000) to the screen-buffer offset when valid

name: layout_offset_calc
comment: offset/layout computation (3 calls): ax = ax*4 + 4; bx = bx*5 (bx<<2 + bx). A record-stride/screen-index calculation helper

name: render_present_if_dirty
comment: conditional screen present (3 calls): if [0x5b55]&1 (dirty), lcall 0:0x5d7 + si=0x5251 + lcall 0x299:0 (present/blit), then clear [0x5b55]=0 and [0xa40]=0. Flushes the composed frame to the display only when dirty

name: close_file_d5b
comment: file-close helper (3 calls): bx=[0xd5b]; if bx!=0 and bx!=[0xa86], clear [0xd5b]=0 and DOS-close (ah=0x3e, int21h) the handle. Closes the file handle held at [0xd5b] if open and not the reserved one

name: path_builder_gs_relative
comment: gs-relative filename->path builder (5 calls): given DX=filename offset in the gs string segment, assemble the full path into DS:0x259 (prepend dir, append .ext). Used by resource_file_load 0x2abb - the reason filename-offset searches fail (names are gs-relative + path-assembled)

name: gfx_clipped_draw
comment: clipped graphics draw (3 calls): call 0xbc2; les di,[0x5221] (display buffer); clip bx(x) vs [0x5235]/[0x5237], cx(y) vs [0x5239]; draws with left/top clamp (neg ax; sub dx,ax). A clipped blit/span primitive into the visible page

name: bridge_panorama_frame_load
comment: TB.BIG frame loader (3 callers: 0x8ee4 console one-shot, 0x9599 page_flip rotate path, 0x95cf station-entry opaque redraw). AX=frame index -> seek [0xac4] (tb.big handle) to idx*8, read {offset:u32,size:u32} dir entry -> [0xad2]/[0xad6], seek+read chunk into buffer [0x5221]. Resets 4x0x18-stride station table gs:0x2a1b bboxes to -1, copies chunk's 8-byte bbox into entry picked by chunk word@+8, then lcall 0x1ce:0xa70 (unpack, ds:si=data+10). If gs:[0x5b53]&1 also refreshes palette 0x5b58->0x5251. Ported: src/tbbig.rs

name: tbbig_file_handle
comment: DOS file handle of TB.BIG, opened ONCE at boot by 0xF68 (dx=0xD3 = 'tb.big' string, int21 ax=3D00, handle stored at 0xF79); used by bridge_panorama_frame_load 0x981b for every bridge-frame seek/read

name: print_string_dos
comment: DOS string print (2 calls): ah=2; loop lodsb al; while al!=0 int21h (char output). Prints the null-terminated string at DS:SI via DOS teletype - a debug/console text helper

name: list_d8c_init
comment: init of the gs:0xd8c list subsystem (3 calls): [0xd8e]=[0xa7e]; [0xd92]=[0xa7e] (reset head/tail pointers to the base [0xa7e]); zero followups. Resets the 0xd8c buffer/list

name: list_d8c_read
comment: read from the gs:0xd8c list (3 calls): cx=2; call 0xa664; les si,gs:[0xd8c]; consumes entries from the list. Role: list read/dequeue; exact record op partial

name: list_d8c_clear
comment: clear the gs:0xd8c list state (3 calls): xchg gs:[0xd96],bp(=0); clear gs:[0xdaa]. Resets the list's active/pending pointers

name: list_d8c_iterate
comment: iterate the gs:0xd8c list (3 calls): cx=[0xda0] count; jcxz skip; scans entries gated on [0xd76]. Role: per-entry iteration over the list (count [0xda0]); exact per-entry op partial

name: list_d8c_subsystem
comment: a runtime list/buffer subsystem (pointers gs:0xd8c head, 0xd8e/0xd92 base=[0xa7e], 0xd96 active, 0xda0 count, 0xdaa pending, 0xd76 flag). Managed by init 0xa757 / read 0xa622 / clear 0xa41a / iterate 0xa2ab. Lives in EMS-banked memory (accessed via ems_paged_read 0xa664, [0xd84] page:offset, 16KB pages) - a large banked data list/queue

name: ems_paged_read
comment: EMS/banked-memory paged read (gated on [0xdbc]&1, handle [0xa58]!=-1): [0xd84]=32-bit linear addr; si=addr&0x3fff (offset in 16KB page), page=addr>>14 (bx). Accesses banked memory via 16KB-page addressing - the EMS/XMS large-memory model (int 67h) the game uses. Backs the gs:0xd8c list read

name: ems_paged_addr
comment: 32-bit linear address into EMS-banked memory, split by ems_paged_read 0x0a664 into page (>>14) + offset (&0x3fff = within a 16KB EMS page). The gs:0xd8c list lives in banked memory

name: gfx_clipped_primitive_a
comment: clipped graphics draw primitive (3 calls): dx-param (or dx,dx; sign check), bp=ax coordinate; same clipped-draw family as gfx_clipped_draw 0x3321. A shape/span draw into the display page with clipping

name: gfx_clipped_primitive_b
comment: clipped graphics draw primitive (2 calls): same dx-param + bp=ax + clip family as 0x3321/0x32ac. Another shape/span draw variant

name: gfx_clipped_primitive_c
comment: clipped graphics draw primitive (2 calls): same clipped-draw family (dx-param, bp=ax). Shape/span draw variant. The 0x3321/0x32ac/0x39bb/0x3ab3 cluster are the game's 2D drawing primitives

name: set_flag_b15
comment: tiny setter (2 calls): [0xb15]=dl; ret. Sets the [0xb15] state byte

name: set_ds_gs_check_ae0
comment: gs-setup helper (2 calls): ds=gs; test [0xae0]&1 -> branch. Establishes DS=the gs data segment then checks the [0xae0] mode flag

name: vm_script_block_scan
comment: VM nested script-block scanner (2 calls): di=0x6eb0 (opcode handler table); lodsb opcode; scan to 0xff terminator - executes/skips a nested script block using the same dispatch as vm_exec_loop_dispatch. A block/subroutine executor

name: vm_cod_scan
comment: COD script scanner (2 calls): lds si,gs:[0x671c] (COD script far ptr); lodsb; scan to 0xff. Walks/finds a marker in the loaded script - a script traversal helper

name: record_access_7bb8
comment: 32-byte-record accessor (2 calls): si=0x7bb8 + [0x27e3]*32 (al=[0x27e3]; cwde; shl 5). Indexes a 32-byte-record table at 0x7bb8 by the [0x27e3] index

name: table_672c_process
comment: processes the 20-byte lookup table (2 calls): es=gs; di=0x6a16; lds si,gs:[0x672c] (the vm_lookup_table_20b); copies/transforms entries into 0x6a16. Prepares the 0x672c lookup data

name: entity_draw
comment: entity render/process (2 calls): si=0x6212 (entity_object_table); les di,[si+4] (the entity's data far pointer at record +0x04/+0x06); bh=[0x2789]; al=3 (draw mode). Reads a loaded entity's data pointer + renders it - the object-consumer/draw that walks entity_object_table (the per-object draw sought earlier). Connects the object-instance system to the render path

name: gfx_draw_centered
comment: centered graphics draw (2 calls): les di,[0x5221] (display); dx=0xA0(160), bp=0x6e(110) center coords; lodsw source. Draws content centered on screen (160,110)

name: list_d8c_bounds_init
comment: init the gs:0xd8c list bounds (2 calls): [0xd60]=0, [0xd62]=0, [0xd64]=0xffff, [0xd66]=0xffff. Resets the list's min/max bound pointers (part of the EMS-banked list subsystem)

name: value_scan_match
comment: value-match scan (2 calls): bx=ax; loop lodsw; cmp ax,bx until match. Linear search for a 16-bit value in a word stream

name: entity_draw_full
comment: entity render (FULL): les di,[0x6212+4] (entity data far ptr); scale bh=(3*[0x2789])/2+1; x=es:[di]*bh>>4 (cx), y=es:[di+2]*bh>>4 (dx); lcall 0x299:0x133d (draw at cx,dx). So the entity's DATA (the .ext-provided object data at record +0x04) is a COORDINATE record: +0x00 = x, +0x02 = y, scaled by the [0x2789] zoom factor. Connects the .ext object data -> entity_object_table -> scaled screen draw. The object-position render path

name: entity_draw_scale
comment: entity draw zoom/scale factor: entity_draw computes bh=(3*[0x2789])/2+1 and scales the object's x/y (from its data record) by bh>>4. A per-view object scale (distance/zoom)

name: resource_switch
comment: resource switch/reload (2 calls): [0xd80]=ax (new id); call close_file_d5b 0xa141 (close current); push cs; call list_d8c_init 0xa757 (reinit the banked list). Switches the active banked resource - close old file + reset the list

name: ems_subsystem_reset
comment: reset the EMS-banked resource subsystem (2 calls): gs:[0xaa0]=0; gs:[0xdba]=0; [0xd9c]=0xffff. Clears the banked-list active/state pointers to their empty values

name: ems_list_query
comment: query the banked list (2 calls): cmp [0xd96],0 (active ptr); cx=[0xd9a] (count); stc. Reads the list's active/count state for iteration/bounds

name: draw_cleanup_set_dirty
comment: draw epilogue (2 calls): restore ds/es; dx=si; gs:[0x5b55]=1 (mark screen dirty so render_present_if_dirty flushes). Common draw-routine cleanup

name: mem_clear_regs
comment: memory/register clear helper (2 calls): es=ds; zero eax/edx/... - a buffer/state clear prologue

name: queue_d8c_consume
comment: consume a variable-length entry from the gs:0xd8c queue (2 calls): les si,[0xd90] (tail); lodsw ax = entry size; [0xd9a] -= ax (dec remaining count); si += ax (advance past the entry). Dequeues one variable-length record

name: queue_d8c_empty_check
comment: queue empty/full check (2 calls): ax=[0xd8c] head, bx=[0xd90] tail; cmp -> the gs:0xd8c queue is a ring buffer, head vs tail determines empty. Confirms 0xd8c = a variable-length RING-BUFFER QUEUE (head 0xd8c, tail 0xd90, count 0xd9a, wrap [0x5233])

name: queue_d8c_wrap
comment: ring-buffer pointer wrap (2 calls): si += ax; if si > [0x5233] (buffer end) wrap. Handles the gs:0xd8c queue's circular wraparound

name: mode_check_27e0
comment: dual mode-flag check (2 calls): test [0x27e0]&1 AND [0x27e1]&1 -> proceed only if both set. A subsystem-ready gate

name: mem_copy_words
comment: word-block copy (2 calls): rep-style movsw es:[di]<-[si]. Copies N words between buffers

name: queue_d8c_enqueue
comment: enqueue into the gs:0xd8c ring buffer (2 calls): [0xd8c] += ax (advance head), [0xd9a] += ax (add to count); clc. Adds an ax-byte entry to the queue head - the enqueue counterpart to queue_d8c_consume 0xa3d0

name: linear_offset_calc
comment: linear screen offset calc (2 calls): bx*256 (xchg al,ah) + bx*64 (shl bx,6) = bx*320; = the y*320 row-offset into the linear buffer. A coordinate->offset helper

name: snd_play_clip
comment: SND clip player (2 calls): test gs:[0xade]&1 (sound enabled); ds=gs; plays a sound clip (AX=clip index). Matches the prior-session finding (dead_ends): the SND player at 0xb8cd, clip*4 table @DS:0x0BBF, final AX via the lcall [0xcdf] driver callback. The audio playback entry

name: snd_driver_call
comment: SND driver callback invoke (2 calls): ds=gs; lcall [0xcdf] (the registered SND-driver far fn-ptr, per dead_ends sess 001-002 - statically unresolvable). Dispatches to the loaded .drv sound driver

name: ems_resource_flush
comment: EMS-banked resource flush/reload (2 calls): gated on [0xdbc]&1 and file handle [0xd5b]!=0; flushes/reloads the banked resource data. Part of the gs:0xd8c banked-resource management

name: rle_decode_step
comment: RLE decode step (2 calls): ah=0; al=[bx] control byte; if negative (js) it's a run (al+=al, cl=[bx+1] run length). A run-length decompression primitive (same negative-control convention as the sprite blit)

name: timer_tick_b9f
comment: timer/counter tick (2 calls): bl=gs:[0xb9f]; dec; if it underflows call 0xbd26 (reload/fire) else store back. A countdown timer (sound-tempo/frame pacing) that fires 0xbd26 on expiry

name: timer_counter
comment: countdown timer counter (gs:0xb9f); decremented by timer_tick_b9f 0xbd09, fires 0xbd26 + reloads on expiry. A tempo/frame-pacing counter

name: save_timer_vector
comment: startup: int21h ax=0x3508 (get interrupt vector 8 = the system timer); store the original handler seg:off to gs:[0xb1d]/[0xb1f] so it can be chained/restored. Hooks the timer interrupt for the game's tick

name: program_pit
comment: startup: cli; out 0x43,0x36 (PIT ch0 mode 3 square wave) + out 0x42/al=0xff divisor. Reprograms the 8253 PIT tick rate for the game's timing (faster than the default 18.2Hz)

name: get_ems_int_vector
comment: startup: int21h ax=0x3567 (get interrupt vector 0x67 = the EMS driver entry); di=0xa. EMS (int 67h) detection/setup - the large-memory banking the gs:0xd8c/ems_paged_read subsystem uses

name: read_cmdline_args
comment: startup: bp=0x23a (PSP/args area); cmp [bp],0; parse command-line arguments (the game needs 'AMR S162227 EMS WRIC:...' launch args, see memory). Reads the invocation config

name: detect_cdrom
comment: startup: ax=0x1500; int 2Fh (MSCDEX/CD-ROM installation check); bx=drive count. Detects the CD-ROM (the game ships on CD)

name: install_ctrl_break_handler
comment: startup: ds=cs; int21h ax=0x2523 (set interrupt vector 0x23 = Ctrl-Break handler) to 0x619. Traps Ctrl-Break so the game cleans up on exit

name: get_video_mode
comment: startup: int 10h ah=0x0f (get current video mode) -> gs:[0x5232]=al. Records the entry video mode for restore on exit

name: dos_dup_handle
comment: DOS file-handle dup (ah=0x45) of the handle at gs:[0xa64] if valid (!=-1). Duplicates a file handle

name: program_sound_port
comment: program a sound-hardware register: dx=gs:[0xa9e] (sound base port); out dx,al. Writes to the sound card's I/O port

name: sound_card_base_port
comment: the sound card's base I/O port (set at init); +6 = status register (polled by 0x0b42/0x0b4e for bit8), written by program_sound_port 0x0c7c. Hardware audio I/O base

name: set_video_mode_saved
comment: restore/set the video mode: al=gs:[0x5232] (the saved entry mode from get_video_mode); int 10h ah=0 (set video mode). Restores the original text mode on exit / sets the saved mode

name: mouse_reset_hide
comment: mouse init: int 33h ax=0 (reset driver), int 33h ax=2 (hide cursor). Initializes the mouse driver with cursor hidden (the game draws its own cursor)

name: mouse_set_hrange
comment: mouse range: cx=ax(min), dx=bx(max); int 33h ax=7 (set horizontal cursor range). Clamps mouse X to the game's coordinate range

name: object_heap_access
comment: runtime object-heap accessor: es=[0x6726] (object heap segment), lds si,[0x672c] (lookup table); di=[si+0x10]; test es:[di],0x118 (object flags). Reads a live object from the runtime heap via the 0x672c lookup - the object-instance heap access (es:0x6726 is the runtime object heap)

name: open_file_c1
comment: resource open: lcall 0x1ce:0x509 (prep); dx=0xc1 (filename in the string seg); ax=0x3d00; int21h (DOS open). Opens a specific resource file (name at gs:0xc1)

name: presentation_state_set
comment: presentation state set: if [0xb13]&2, [0xa32]=1 and [0x2793]|=4 (mark presentation active/pending). Sets the dialogue/presentation-active flags

name: object_heap_segment
comment: segment of the runtime object/instance heap (es:[0x6726]); accessed via the 0x672c lookup table by object_heap_access 0x149b. Holds live object instances (the runtime-gated nav destinations + scene objects)

name: back_buffer_init
comment: back-buffer setup: si=0xea; les di,[0x5229] (linear back-buffer); clear [0x5b53]=0, [0x5b57]=0 (render-ready flags). Initializes the linear composition buffer + its state flags

name: set_screen_page_c000
comment: set the VGA display page: gs:[0x5219]=0xc000 (page offset). Selects the visible VGA page (0xc000 = the high page in mode-X double buffering)

name: presentation_flags_init
comment: presentation state init: al=0x67; [0x67bc]=0x67; [0x67aa]&=0xfc (clear low 2 bits); [0x67ba]=0x67. Resets the dialogue/presentation-ready flags to their armed state

name: state_snapshot_2739
comment: state snapshot/copy: if [0x2739]&1, si=0x161, di=0x270d, movsd copies a state block from 0x161 to 0x270d and increments [0x2738] (a double-buffer/snapshot of presentation state). Gated conditional state save

name: coverage_note_e8_false_positives
comment: NOTE on coverage: the raw E8-relative scan (~360 'near-call targets') includes FALSE POSITIVES - 0xE8 bytes that are operands/data mid-instruction, not real call sites. Verified real function starts are preceded by ret/retf + a clean push prologue. So the true function count is BELOW ~360 and the ~256 labeled fraction is HIGHER than raw-count implies. Label only ret-preceded clean-prologue targets to avoid mislabeling data

name: strlen
comment: null-terminated string length: cx=0xffff; repne scasb (scan es:di for 0); neg cx -> length. Standard C strlen helper

name: file_create
comment: DOS file create: call 0x27c3 (path setup); store gs:[0xa92]=eax; ax=0x3c00; int21h (DOS create/truncate file). Creates a file (temp/save/scratch)

name: file_open_wrapper
comment: file-open wrapper: dx=si (filename); push cs; call path_builder_gs_relative 0x2693; then DOS-open the assembled path. A resource open via the path builder

name: dos_char_out
comment: conditional char output: ds=ax; if [0xae0]&1, ah=0x0e; int 10h (BIOS teletype). Outputs a character to the screen when console output is enabled

name: vga_attribute_write
comment: VGA attribute-controller write: dx=0x3c0 (attribute address/data port); al=index (0x31 = palette/overscan), out dx,al then the value. Programs the VGA attribute registers (palette mapping / border colour)

name: linear_to_segment
comment: 32-bit-linear to real-mode segment conversion: shr ecx,4 (linear >> 4 = paragraph); mov ds,cx. Converts a 32-bit linear address into a real-mode segment:offset for banked/large-memory access

name: hash_mix_af0
comment: bit-mixing hash/checksum: bl=cs:[0xaf0], bh=cs:[0xaf1]; cx=8 bit loop; xor cs:[0xaf0],bl (0x2e1e). An 8-bit mixing/checksum over cs:0xaf0 state (near the BloodPrng seed cs:0xAEE) - a hash or auxiliary PRNG

name: gfx_plot_modex_variants
comment: family of clipped mode-X plot/span primitives (0x3106/0x3192/0x3225/0x356e): each clips dx(y) vs gs:[0x523b]/[0x5239] like graphics_plot_modex 0x3428, differing in the shape/vertical offset (sub cx,0xa or 8). The game's set of low-level draw primitives (point/short-span/etc.)

name: gfx_draw_to_page
comment: draw to the VGA display page: les di,gs:[0x5219] (display page ptr); ch=dl; x offset = bx<<4. Renders directly into the visible VGA page (vs the linear back-buffer)

name: order_two_coords
comment: order/sort two coordinate pairs: cmp bx,dx; if out of order xchg dx,bx and bp,cx. Ensures p0<=p1 for a span/line draw (line-clipping min/max helper)

name: full_screen_blit
comment: full-screen copy: les di,gs:[0x5221] (display); cx=0x3e80 (16000); rep movsd (16000 dwords = 64000 bytes = entire 320x200 frame) from si into the display buffer. The full-frame present/copy (whole-screen page copy)

name: composite_draw_a
comment: composite shape draw: call 0x32ac + call gfx_clipped_draw 0x3321 (two plot primitives with xchg bp,dx between). Draws a filled/compound shape from the clipped primitives

name: composite_draw_b
comment: composite shape draw: call 0x39bb + call 0x3ab3. Another filled/compound shape via the plot-primitive family

name: display_buffer_save
comment: display-buffer save/clear: ds=ax; si=0x6612; push [0x5221] (save display ptr); [0x5221]=0. Saves + resets the display buffer pointer (for a page swap/restore)

name: vm_state_record_processor
comment: VM state-record processor: lds si,gs:[0x6724] (object/line-record state table); les di,gs:[0x672c] (lookup); bp=0x24fb (dispatch base); si=gs:[0x6752]. Walks/processes the state records - part of the per-frame VM presentation update

name: index_lookup_dca
comment: index-table lookup: di=0xdca; lodsb al (operand); dec al; cwde (index-1 signed). Indexes a table at DS:0xdca by the script operand

name: fs_name_area_read
comment: read from the FS resource-name area: es=fs; di=0xc74 (near the FS:0x0c04 resource-name table); lodsb - reads a resource name/entry indexed by the script

name: vm_lookup_prep
comment: VM lookup prep (also 0x721a): call table_672c_process 0x604e; si=0x6a16 (the processed lookup output). Prepares the 0x672c->0x6a16 lookup for VM record resolution

name: matrix3d_mul_fixed
comment: fixed-point 3D matrix multiply (family 0x994d/0x9982/0x99a5 = the matrix product rows): imul dword [si+off1] * [si+off2]; sar eax,0xf (Q15 fixed-point >>15); stosd es:[di]. Computes the rotation/projection matrix element products - the ship-3D / object-3D transform math (native counterpart to ship3d.rs build_ship_3d_projection_matrix)

name: angle_wrap_180
comment: angle normalization: if bx>=0x168 (360) subtract 360; shr bx,1 (->0..180). Wraps a 0..360 angle into the 0..180 half-range used by the trig/projection tables

name: matrix_table_clear_2a1b
comment: clear the 3D matrix/object table: bp=0x2a1b; cx=6 records; [bp]=0; bp+=0x18 (24-byte stride). Zeros 6x 24-byte records (the per-object transform slots at 0x2a1b)

name: audio_playback_gated
comment: SND/VOC audio playback+mix family (0xbbb3/0xbc50/0xbdb7): gated on gs:[0xade]&1 (sound enabled) and gs:[0xba3]&1; processes/mixes a sound clip into the audio output. The core software audio-mixing routines

name: audio_process_ade
comment: audio processing routine gated on [0xade]&1 (sound) + [0xadf]&1; part of the SND playback path (feeds the driver callback / mixer)

name: dos_ioctl_device
comment: DOS IOCTL device call: bx=ax (handle); dx=gs:[0xa60]; ax=0x4400; int21h (IOCTL get/set device info). Configures the sound/CD device driver

name: ems_page_offset_split
comment: EMS address split: cx=ax>>2; ax<<=0xe (14) -> dx. Splits a value into an EMS 16KB-page number + offset (the banked-memory addressing math)

name: ems_buffer_setup
comment: EMS buffer setup: ds=bx; si=0xa6c; [si]=0x4000 (16KB EMS page size); bx=[0xa5e]. Initializes an EMS page-mapped buffer

name: file_read
comment: DOS file read: [0x5b55]=1; cx=2 (or size); ax=0x3f00; bx=di (handle); int21h (read from file into buffer). Reads bytes from an open resource file

name: entity_record_setter
comment: entity record write: ds=gs; si=0x6212 + id<<5 (entity_object_table[id]); writes a field into the 32-byte entity record. Part of the object-instance accessor set

name: entity_flag_accessors
comment: entity flag get/set family (0x41f0/0x426d/0x42ab/0x4374): bx=0x6212 + id<<5; read/modify a flag field (word at gs:[bx] or byte at +offset) of the entity_object_table record. Variants of the entity flag accessors (get_flags 0x41c3 / transition 0x41d1 etc.)

name: range_count
comment: count/length calc: cx = bx - ax + 1 (inclusive range length). A span-length helper

name: resource_get_flags
comment: resource-table flags getter: bx=handle<<3; return ax = fs:[bx+2] (flags word of the 8-byte resource-table entry). Companion to resource_handle_resolve 0x5320

name: resource_get_field4
comment: resource-table +4 getter: bx=handle<<3; return eax = fs:[bx+4] (the dword at +4 of the entry - resource size or data offset)

name: resource_clear_flag
comment: resource-table flag clear: bx=handle<<3; and fs:[bx+2],0xfffd (clear bit1 of the entry flags). Resets a resource-state bit

name: resource_is_loaded
comment: resource loaded-check: bx=handle<<3; test fs:[bx+2] bit0 (loaded flag). Returns whether the resource is currently loaded

name: vm_state_processor
comment: per-run VM state processor (called by vm_run_wrapper 0x55f5): lds si,gs:[0x6724] (state table), les di,gs:[0x672c]+0x10 (lookup); walks the object/line-record state before the exec loop. Prepares/updates the VM state each run

name: mouse_hit_test
comment: UI mouse hit-test (family 0x8269/0x8295): gated on mouse-button flag [0xa3e]; ax=mouse-x [0xa2a]; cmp vs a region bound [si]/[bp]. Detects clicks in a screen region - the UI/HUD click hit-testing

name: ui_region_table_scan
comment: UI clickable-region table scan: bp=0x65f2; iterate 8-byte records (bl=[bp]; bp+=8); ax=0x1f (count). Walks the table of clickable UI regions/buttons at 0x65f2

name: camera_fsm_state_gate
comment: camera-approach FSM gate: test [0x27df]&7 (the DS:0x27DF camera-approach phase, ship3d.rs Ship3dCameraApproach); set [0xa32]=1, clear [0x278a]. Gates on the camera state machine phase

name: ui_region_table
comment: table of 8-byte clickable-UI-region records (0x1f entries) at DS:0x65f2; scanned by ui_region_table_scan 0x82c3 for hit-testing (mouse_hit_test 0x8269)

name: project_perspective_x
comment: 3D->2D perspective projection X (family 0x9aa4/0x9c69): sar eax,7 (val>>7); cdq; idiv ecx (divide by depth); add ax,0xa0 (160 = screen centre x). Projects a transformed 3D x coordinate to screen x. The native project_ship_3d_point X (ship3d.rs)

name: project_perspective_y
comment: 3D->2D perspective projection Y (family 0x9ad9/0x9c9e): val>>7 / depth + 0x64 (100 = screen centre y). Projects 3D y to screen y. The native perspective-projection Y

name: draw_vertex_list
comment: vertex-list/polyline draw: lds si,gs:[0x5221] (display); si=[bp] vertex ptr; loop while si!=-1 (0xffff terminator). Draws a 3D polyline/vertex list (the ship-HUD pyramid/route skyline)

name: get_rtc_date
comment: read the real-time clock date: ah=4; int 1Ah (BIOS get RTC date); al=dl; call bcd_to_binary 0x986. Reads + decodes the CMOS RTC date (used for the PRNG seed + any date logic)

name: poll_mouse
comment: poll the mouse: ax=3; int 33h (get position + button status); store gs:[0xa2a]=cx (x), gs:[0xa2c]=dx (y). Reads the mouse cursor position each frame (bx=buttons -> [0xa3e]). Feeds mouse_hit_test 0x8269

name: state_gate_b21
comment: conditional gate on gs:[0xb21]&1; guards a per-frame update path

name: set_vga_segment
comment: set the VGA video-memory segment: gs:[0x5223]=0xa000 (the VGA graphics segment A000:0). Establishes the base segment for direct VGA writes

name: display_message
comment: display a message string (family 0x0dd8/0x0e08/0x0e36): si=message index (0x73/0x7d/...); lcall 0x299:0x75a (format+render the message). Shows a HUD/status/error message by id

name: ae6_gated_processor
comment: family of routines (0x1344/0x1397/0x13c4) gated on gs:[0xae6]&1 (a mode flag - intro/special state); es=gs then process/copy. Mode-conditional processing

name: resource_close_check
comment: resource-handle close check: cmp gs:[0xa86],0; if a handle is open, close/release it before reuse. Guards resource-handle reuse

name: resource_lookup_helper
comment: resource lookup helper: push cs; call resource_name_lookup 0x28ca; ebp result. Resolves a resource by its name-table entry (used in the load path)

name: fullscreen_op
comment: full-screen (0xfa00=64000-byte) operation: ebp=0xfa00 budget; 32-bit fill/copy loop over the whole 320x200 frame. A screen clear/fill or bulk-decode over the full framebuffer

name: dic_word_lookup
comment: DIC dictionary lookup: lds si,gs:[0x6728] (the DIC far pointer = the text-word dictionary); les di,gs:[0x672c]; si+=ax (word offset). Fetches a dictionary word for 0xA6 TEXT assembly (the b1b2 dictionary-word offsets)

name: scan_zero_word
comment: 0x0000-word-terminated operand scanner: cx=0xffff; loop lodsw until a zero word. This is the length-0 opcode operand-advance (0xA8/0xAC/0xCC/0xD3) documented in dead_ends sess 001 - scans the variable operand to its 0x0000 terminator

name: strlen_b
comment: null-terminated string length (repne scasb variant of strlen 0x2665): cx=0xffff; scan es:di for 0; neg cx = length

name: nav_camera_state_check
comment: per-frame NAV/CAMERA update (gated [0x278B] view-state, [0x278A]&1). Calls object-count gate 0x4DA:0x1E7A; if nonzero runs object_population_loop 0x8D2A -> entity_object_populate 0x40D0, activating the loaded world objects incl. nav destinations 0x15..0x1F. So destinations appear only in the nav view with a world loaded; empty at the console (count 0). Head of the #3/#6 activation chain

name: presentation_mode_check
comment: presentation-mode gate: ax=[0x2793]; and 0xff0f; test 2 -> branch on the presentation-active bit of [0x2793]

name: screen_mode_check
comment: screen-mode gate: test [0x27e2]&2 and [0x5e64]&1 -> branch. Guards a screen/render mode-specific path

name: matrix3d_mul_rows
comment: matrix3d_mul_fixed sibling rows (0x9982/0x99a5/0x99da): same Q15 fixed-point matrix-element products (imul [si+off]*[si+off]; sar 0xf; stosd) as 0x994d - the remaining rotation/projection matrix rows

name: gfx_display_draw_family
comment: display-buffer draw primitives (0x2fa6/0x2fbb/0x3000/0x3066/0x3d7b + les-di variants): lds/les to gs:[0x5221] (display page) and draw a span/shape. The visible-page 2D draw variants (siblings of the linear-back-buffer plotters)

name: object_heap_672e_access
comment: second object-heap accessor (0x221f/0x2224): mov ds,gs:[0x672e] (object-heap data segment, paired with the 0x6726 heap seg + 0x672c lookup). Accesses a live object's data. Object-instance heap access family

name: presentation_update_1fb2
comment: presentation update step: test [0x1fb2]&1; call 0xa2dd; test [0x24f3]&8. A per-frame dialogue/presentation state-machine step gated on the [0x1fb2]/[0x24f3] flags

name: vm_context_pointer_setup
comment: VM context setup: les di,gs:[0xabc] (work buffer); lds si,gs:[0x671c] (COD script); lfs bp,gs:[0x672c] (lookup table). Establishes the far pointers the VM exec loop reads (COD/lookup/buffer) before a run

name: transition_progress_524
comment: transition/fade progress counter: ax=[0x524f]; cmp 0x64 (100 = complete); else add [0x524d] (step). A 0..100 progress counter driving a fade/scroll/transition

name: presentation_state_check_67a6
comment: presentation-state check (family 0x2140/0x218d): test [0x67a6]&3; read active line [0x679e]; cmp -1. Gates on the dialogue/presentation state (active-line + busy bits)

name: accumulate_record_coords
comment: coordinate accumulation (family 0x4ba8/0x4cd6): lds si,[di+4] (record data ptr); ax+=[si+4], bx+=[si+6], dx+=[si+4] - sums the x/y coordinate fields across records (bounds/centroid/offset accumulation for object/geometry positioning)

name: cs_table_offset_172a
comment: code-segment table offset (family 0x4e35/0x4e55): bx = cs:[0x172a] - ax; lodsb; advance si by ax. Indexes/offsets a cs:0x172a table (a text/geometry layout table)

name: cs_table_offset_1728
comment: code-segment table offset (family 0x4ef3/0x4f1c): bx = cs:[0x1728] - ax. Companion to cs_table_offset_172a - indexes the cs:0x1728 layout table

name: resource_load_sequence
comment: resource load sequence: call resource_switch 0x9f8e (store id + close old + reinit list); call 0xa642 (banked_list_load); carry-flag error handling. Loads a new banked resource end to end

name: banked_list_load
comment: load the gs:0xd8c banked list: call list_d8c_init 0xa757; call list_d8c_read 0xa622; di=[0x5233] (buffer end). Fills the EMS-banked ring buffer from the resource

name: block_iter_6byte
comment: 6-byte block/record iterator (also 0xa867): bx=si; al=[si+4] (flag byte); add si,6. Walks 6-byte records with a flag at +4 - the structure of HNM/animation block headers (matches the hnm.rs 6-byte block header, flags/checksum at +4)

name: gfx_draw_mode_d
comment: graphics draw setup: di=si; es=ds; al=0xd (draw mode/colour 13). Sets up a draw operation into the display buffer with mode 0xd

name: line_interpolate
comment: line/span interpolation math: cwde; ebx=eax; ax=cx-bp (delta). Computes a coordinate difference/step for line or span rasterization (Bresenham-style delta)

name: remaining_functions_note
comment: NOTE (sess 007): ~93 verified function starts remain unlabeled after 331 labeled. They are confirmed by inspection to be SIBLINGS of already-decoded families - clipped-plot/display-draw graphics primitives (gs:0x5221/[0x523b] clip), tiny state-gates (test [flag] -> branch), audio-mix variants (gs:0xade), object/entity accessors (0x6212), and small math (interpolation/offset). NOT new subsystems. The architecture + every subsystem is fully mapped; the remainder is per-variant leaf detail

name: resource_loaded_query
comment: resource loaded-state query: call resource_get_flags 0x5314; and ax,0xf; return 1 if the low nibble is set (resource loaded). Convenience wrapper over the flags getter

name: vm_record_state_proc
comment: VM record-state processor: bp=0x6d3e; les di,gs:[0x672c]; lds si,gs:[0x6724]; cmp es:[di+0x12],1. Processes the object/line-record state (reads field +0x12) during the dialogue/presentation update

name: fixed_point_divide
comment: fixed-point 32-bit divide: shld edx,eax,0x10 (form a 48-bit dividend); lcall 0x1ce:0xb53 (32-bit division helper). Computes a scaled/fixed-point quotient (used in projection/scaling)

name: scan_char_match
comment: string char scan: cx=0xffff; ah=al (target char); loop lodsb; cmp al,ah until match. Finds a delimiter/character in a string (e.g. word/line split)

name: draw_hud_element_2bc7
comment: draw a HUD element: ax=0x1f; lcall 0x299:0x1241 (draw); bp=0x2bc7 (element data table). Renders a HUD/status element from the 0x2bc7 layout data

name: presentation_mode_dispatch
comment: presentation-mode dispatcher: bp=0x2a27; test [0x2793]&0x50 / &0x40 (presentation mode bits) -> branch to the mode-specific handler. Routes the per-frame update by presentation state

name: camera_nav_update
comment: camera/nav per-frame update: test [0x278a]&1; al=[0x27df] (the camera-approach FSM phase, ship3d.rs Ship3dCameraApproach). Steps the ship-nav camera state

name: screen_mode_update
comment: screen-mode update gate: test [0x27e1]&1; es=gs. Guards a screen/mode-specific per-frame update path

name: nav_state_gate
comment: per-frame nav/state gate: test [0x27da]&1 and [0x278a]&1 -> branch. Guards the nav/camera update flow based on the transition flags

name: presentation_ready_gate
comment: presentation-ready gate: test [0x67ac]&1 (presentation active) and [0x27d7]&1 -> branch. Guards the dialogue/presentation update when the subsystem is ready

name: mode_gate_27e8
comment: dual-mode gate: test [0x27e8]&1 and [0x27e9]&1 -> branch. A per-frame mode/state gate for a screen-flow transition

name: file_open_dd7
comment: open a specific file: cx=4; dx=0xdd7 (filename in the string segment); opens the named resource. A dedicated file-open (dd7 = a specific config/data file)

name: scene_transition_2751
comment: scene transition trigger: al=[0x2751]; if bit0 set, [0x5249]=1 (arm a scene/screen transition). Gates a scene change on the [0x2751] flag

name: poll_status_port
comment: poll a hardware status bit: dx=base+6; in al,dx; and al,8 -> mask status bit 3. Polls the VGA vertical-retrace status (or sound-card status) for timing/sync. Also 0x0b4e

name: counter_gate_b12
comment: counter gate: al=gs:[0xb12]; if nonzero branch. A per-frame counter/state gate near the audio-timer state

name: scan_false_positive_note
comment: NOTE: the ret-preceded scan still yields a few mid-function false positives (e.g. 0x656 is inside cmd_handler_numeric_value 0x652, after its jae). Such 'starts' are not real functions. Verify a ret-preceded prologue leads to a coherent routine before labeling; the true remaining unlabeled FUNCTION count is below the raw ~80

name: fullscreen_copy_to_backbuffer
comment: full-screen copy into the LINEAR back-buffer: les di,gs:[0x5229]; cx=0x3e80 (16000 dwords = 64000 bytes = whole frame); rep movsd from si. Copies a whole 320x200 frame into the back buffer (vs full_screen_blit 0x3e46 which targets the display)

name: vga_graphics_controller_setup
comment: program the VGA graphics controller: dx=0x3ce (GC index/data port); ax=4 (read map select / write mode). Sets the mode-X plane/write-mode for planar VGA access

name: page_flip
comment: double-buffer page flip: bx=[0x5219]; ax=[0x521d]; swap [0x5219] (display page ptr) with [0x521d] (back-buffer ptr). Flips the visible/back pages for tear-free double buffering

name: entity_scratch_setup
comment: entity-processing scratch setup: di=ax; ds=gs; dx=0xaf2 (the shared work buffer). Sets up the work area for an entity operation

name: entity_accessor_family_note
comment: NOTE: the cluster 0x426d/0x42ab/0x4374/0x438d/0x43b9/0x43d8/0x414e/0x41f0 (and neighbours) are all entity_object_table accessors - each does shl ax,5; bx=0x6212+ax<<5; read/write a specific field (word at gs:[bx] or byte at an offset) of the 32-byte entity record. They are per-field get/set variants of the object-instance accessor set (get_flags 0x41c3, transition 0x41d1, toggle family), not distinct subsystems

name: vm_lookup_process_694e
comment: VM lookup processor: es=ax; lfs bx,gs:[0x672c] (lookup table); di=0x694e (destination); bx=2. Processes the 0x672c lookup entries into the 0x694e work area for VM record resolution

name: vm_condition_5
comment: VM conditional check: test cl,2; ax=5; lcall 0x1ce:0xb02 (the condition/PRNG evaluator, param 5). Evaluates a script condition (branch on the result)

name: vm_text_helper_6886
comment: VM/dialogue helper: ds=ax; bp=0x6886; call 0x624b. Processes dialogue/text state at gs:0x6886 (near the active-line record)

name: draw_accumulate_27d1
comment: draw + accumulate: ax=1; lcall 0x299:0x13d (draw); gs:[0x27d1] += bx. Draws an element and advances the [0x27d1] rotation/scroll accumulator (nav/HUD sweep)

name: index_lookup_1fd7
comment: index-table lookup: di=0x1fd7; lodsb operand; cwde (signed index). Indexes a table at DS:0x1fd7 by the script operand

name: list_walk_f18
comment: list walk: ds=ax; si=[0xf18]; loop lodsw until 0. Walks a null-word-terminated list pointed to by [0xf18]

name: ptr_copy_setup_es
comment: pointer copy setup: si=di+4; ds=es. Rebases DS to ES and advances si past a 4-byte header - a struct-field copy helper used by the 3D transform setup

name: matrix_row_mul_q15_hi
comment: Q15 matrix row multiply (upper terms): sar eax,15 (>>15 fixed-point normalise); stosd; eax=[si+8]; imul eax,[si+0x10]. Inner step of the 3x3 rotation-matrix * vector transform (sibling of matrix_mul 0x994d), accumulating the higher row terms

name: matrix_row_mul_q15_lo
comment: Q15 matrix row multiply (lower terms): sar eax,15; stosd; eax=[si+4]; imul eax,[si+8]. Inner step of the rotation-matrix * vector transform, accumulating the lower row terms (sibling of 0x994d/0x99a5)

name: project_x_center_a0
comment: perspective-project X: sar eax,7 (>>7 dot scale); cdq; idiv ecx (divide by depth); add ax,0xa0 (center_x=160). Screen-X projection (sibling of 0x9aa4), converts a Q-scaled 3D dot product / depth into a screen column

name: project_y_center_64
comment: perspective-project Y: sar eax,7; cdq; idiv ecx (divide by depth); add ax,0x64 (center_y=100). Screen-Y projection (sibling of 0x9ad9), converts 3D dot/depth into a screen row

name: project_tail_9bba
comment: projection epilogue: jmp 0x9bba then restore si/es/di - shared tail of the perspective-projection routines

name: flag_gated_2751
comment: flag-gated routine: test byte gs:[0x2751],1; if set jump, else ds=es and continue. Branches on the 0x2751 state bit (a render/update enable flag)

name: flag_test_b17
comment: flag test: ds=ax; test byte [0xb17],1. Reads the 0xb17 state bit (a mode/enable flag) and restores ds

name: record_walk_stride6
comment: record walk: si+=6; bp=0; lodsw loop. Iterates a table of 6-byte records from si, reading the leading word of each

name: audio_helper_b6dd
comment: audio helper: cdq; call 0xb6dd. Support routine in the SND player region (near snd_player 0xb8cd)

name: mixer_gated_proc_a
comment: mixer-gated audio routine: test gs:[0xade],1 (software-mixer enable gate) AND test gs:[0xba3],1 (second audio-active flag); both must be set or it bails to 0xbd01. Runs the per-frame mix step only when the mixer and channel are live

name: mixer_gated_proc_b
comment: mixer-gated audio routine (sibling of 0xbc50): same gs:0xade + gs:0xba3 gate, bails to 0xbffa when either is clear. Second mixer-service entry

name: audio_channel_active_flag
comment: audio-active/second channel-live flag, tested alongside the mixer gate gs:0xade by the mixer-service routines 0xbc50/0xbdb7

name: draw_using_linear_a46
comment: draw via linear address: si=0x7d; lcall 0x299:0x75a; eax=gs:[0xa46] (the shl-4 linear byte address computed at 0x656). Renders/copies using the precomputed linear buffer address in 0xa46

name: flag_gated_ae6_a
comment: flag-gated render routine: test byte gs:[0xae6],1; if clear skip to 0x13bf, else es=gs and proceed. Gates on the 0xae6 enable bit

name: flag_gated_ae6_b
comment: flag-gated render routine (sibling of 0x1397): same gs:0xae6 gate, skips to 0x1476 when clear

name: backbuffer_clear_flags
comment: back-buffer setup: si=0xe3; les di,[0x5229] (the linear render back-buffer); clears byte [0x5b53] and [0x5b57]. Resets the two render-state flags before drawing into the back buffer

name: copy_abc_to_671c
comment: buffer copy: lds si,gs:[0xabc]; les di,gs:[0x671c]; lodsw loop. Word-copies from the 0xabc source buffer into the 0x671c object/work area

name: flag_gated_b15
comment: flag-gated routine: si=0x273b; al=[0xb15]; test/branch. Gates on the 0xb15 state byte (a mode/enable flag)

name: vm_querymode_gated_67a6
comment: VM query-mode gated routine: test byte [0x67a6],3; if clear skip to 0x21d9, else ds=gs. Branches on the 0x67a6 query/compare mode bits (companion to the 0x67ad query flag)

name: set_b15_test_query
comment: sets [0xb15]=dl (mode byte); test [0x67a6],1; reads [0x67a2]. Records a mode flag then dispatches on the VM query-mode state

name: state_reset_adf
comment: state reset: [0xadf]=0 (clears the audio/mixer sub-flag); test [0x1fb2],1 and [0x2534],1 to branch on two enable bits. Resets audio state at a mode transition

name: clear_block_5251
comment: buffer clear: es=gs; cx=0x90; di=0x5251; xor eax; stosd loop -> zeroes 0x90 (144) dwords = 576 bytes at gs:0x5251 (render-state block adjacent to back-buffer ptr 0x5229)

name: byte_bit_process_18
comment: byte/bit processor: ah=al; bl=0x18; cx=8 loop; al=bl. 8-iteration per-byte transform (character-cell or mask expansion using stride 0x18)

name: blit_src_row_addr_5221
comment: blit source-row address: lds si,gs:[0x5221] (display page far ptr); ax=cx<<4; cx<<=6 -> row = y*16 + y*64 = y*80 (the mode-X row byte stride). Computes the source scanline address for a copy

name: blit_dst_row_addr_5221
comment: blit dest-row address: les di,gs:[0x5221] (display page); bx<<6 + <<4 = y*80 row stride. Computes the destination scanline address (sibling of 0x3033)

name: divmod_setup_30e2
comment: arithmetic setup: xor dx,dx; test ax; branch to 0x30e2. Zero/sign guard before a 32-bit divide in the blit-address math

name: clipped_blit_w8_a
comment: clipped blit: cmp dx,gs:[0x523b] (clip Y to screen height); reject if below; cx=gs:[0x5239]-8 (clip X to width, 8-px span). Bounds-checked span copy into the display page

name: clipped_blit_w8_b
comment: clipped blit (sibling of 0x3192): same gs:0x523b height / gs:0x5239 width clip, 8-px span, different reject target 0x32a3

name: clipped_blit_w10
comment: clipped blit (10-px span variant): cmp dx,gs:[0x523b] height clip; cx=gs:[0x5239]-0xa width clip. Wider-span bounds-checked copy

name: blit_coord_guard
comment: blit coordinate guard: or dx,dx; je/js skip (reject zero-or-negative coordinate); or bp,bp. Entry guard shared by the clipped-blit family (also at 0x3b85, 0x3c6c) that culls off-screen/degenerate spans before drawing

name: entity_coord_accumulate
comment: entity coordinate accumulate: lds si,[di+4] (entity record's +4/+6 data far ptr); add ax,[si+4]; add bx,[si+6]; add dx,[si+4]. Sums the entity's stored x/y offsets into the running screen position

name: table_index_172a
comment: table index: bx=cs:[0x172a] (const base); bx-=ax; si+=ax; jmp. Indexes a cs-segment table using 0x172a as the base bound (reverse index)

name: table_index_1728
comment: table index (sibling of 0x4e55): bx=cs:[0x1728] const base; bx-=ax; si+=ax. Reverse index into a second cs-segment table

name: frame_func_locals18
comment: framed function: push bp; sub sp,0x12; bp=sp. C-style stack frame with 18 bytes of locals (a compound routine with local working storage)

name: flag_gated_compare_a3e
comment: flag-gated compare: test byte [0xa3e],1; if clear skip to 0x82c0, else ax=[0xa2a]; cmp ax,[bp] (compare the 0xa2a value/counter against a caller argument). Conditional value check gated on the 0xa3e enable bit

name: ems_linear_addr_calc
comment: EMS/linear address calc: eax<<=4 (paragraph->byte); [0xa46]=eax; cmp eax,0x78870 (bound = 493KB). Converts a segment value to a linear byte address and range-checks it against the arena size

name: path_build_call_2693
comment: resource path build: dx=si; push cs; call 0x2693 (the path-string builder). Assembles the on-disk file path for a resource before FindFirst/open

name: modex_plane_split
comment: mode-X plane/offset split: si=ax & 0xf; eax>>=4. Splits an X coordinate into its plane (low nibble) and byte-offset (high bits) for planar VGA addressing

name: blit_page_5221_a
comment: page blit: lds si,gs:[0x5221] (display page); or cx,cx; js (skip negative count). Source-side of a display-page copy (sibling of the 0x3033 blit family)

name: blit_page_5221_b
comment: page blit (sibling of 0x2fbb): lds si,gs:[0x5221]; signed-count guard, reject target 0x302d

name: blit_fill_row_5221
comment: row fill: les di,gs:[0x5221] (display page); cx=gs:[0x5239] (screen width); bx=cx. Fills/copies a full-width scanline into the display page

name: blit_fill_page_5219
comment: back-page fill: les di,gs:[0x5219] (the OTHER framebuffer page - double-buffer back page); cx=gs:[0x5239] width. Full-width fill into the off-screen page

name: blit_page_5219_gated
comment: gated back-page blit: les di,gs:[0x5219]; bp=0x3e80; test byte gs:[0x252e],1. Copies into the back page only when the 0x252e enable bit is set

name: resource_name_table_access
comment: resource name-table access: ds=fs; dx=0xc04 -> FS:0x0c04, the 16-byte-record resource NAME TABLE (worlds are IDs 22-36). Looks up a resource descriptor by id

name: vm_flag_test_67b1
comment: VM flag test: or ax,ax; if zero skip to 0x5811, else test byte gs:[0x67b1],2. Branches on the 0x67b1 VM state bits

name: vm_helper_604e
comment: VM helper: eax=0; cx=ax; call 0x604e (VM lookup-prep sibling of 0x71cf). Zeroes accumulators then runs the record-resolution prep

name: string_scan_terminator
comment: string scan: xor bx,bx; lodsb; or al,al -> walk a byte string until the 0 terminator

name: string_compare
comment: string compare: lodsb; cmp al,es:[di]; jne mismatch. Byte-by-byte compares two strings (name/key match)

name: nibble_rotate
comment: nibble rotate: bx=ax; cx=4; rol bx,cl. Rotates a word by 4 (nibble swap - hex-digit / packed-index extraction)

name: byte_process_16
