comment: byte processor: bx=ax; ah=0x18; cx=0x10 (16-iteration loop). Per-bit/byte transform over 16 units with stride 0x18

name: entity_flag_accessor_1
comment: entity_object_table accessor: ax<<=5; bx=0x6212+ax; read/write a field of the 32-byte entity record (member of the entity_flag_accessors family)

name: entity_flag_accessor_2
comment: entity_object_table accessor (0x6212+id<<5) - entity_flag_accessors family member

name: entity_flag_accessor_3
comment: entity_object_table accessor (0x6212+id<<5) - entity_flag_accessors family member

name: entity_flag_accessor_4
comment: entity_object_table accessor: ax<<=5; di=0x6212+ax (di variant) - entity_flag_accessors family member

name: entity_flag_accessor_5
comment: entity_object_table accessor (0x6212+id<<5) - entity_flag_accessors family member

name: entity_flag_accessor_6
comment: entity_object_table accessor (0x6212+id<<5) - entity_flag_accessors family member

name: blit_coord_guard_b
comment: blit coordinate guard (sibling of 0x339e): or dx,dx; je/js 0x3c63 cull; or bp,bp. Off-screen/degenerate-span cull

name: blit_coord_guard_c
comment: blit coordinate guard (sibling of 0x339e): si=ax; or dx,dx; je/js 0x3d71 cull

name: ds_es_rebase_gs
comment: helper prologue: bp=ax; ds=es=gs. Rebases the segment registers to the GS work arena before a data operation

name: cs_data_ptr_setup
comment: cs-relative data pointer setup: ds=cs; si=0x1c6; si+=0xb. Points si at a cs-segment constant table (offset 0x1c6+0xb) for read

name: toggle_cs_byte_af0
comment: self-modifying state toggle: xor byte cs:[0xaf0],bl; or dx,dx. Flips bits in the cs:0xaf0 state byte (a persistent mode toggle stored in the code segment)

name: accumulate32_loop
comment: 32-bit accumulate loop: add ecx,eax; cmp ecx,edx; jl. Running 32-bit sum with an upper-bound compare (fixed-point / offset accumulation)

name: clear_regs_setup
comment: register-clear setup: xor eax,eax; ebx=eax; bx=si. Zero-extends si into ebx and clears eax before a 32-bit computation

name: mem_seg_setup_es8
comment: memory segment setup: si=0; ax=es; ax+=8. Advances a segment value by 8 paragraphs (skips a segment header / MCB) during allocation

name: palette_upload_wrapper
comment: palette upload: ds=es=gs; si=0x5b58 (game_palette_dac_buffer); lcall 0x299:0 -> vga_palette_write 0x2f90, which rep-outsb 768 bytes to the VGA DAC. Pushes the current game palette to hardware

name: flag_gated_259b
comment: flag-gated routine: test byte [0x259b],1; if clear skip to 0x1b4a, else si=0x259d. Branches on the 0x259b enable bit and points at the 0x259d parameter block

name: far_call_wrapper_299_deb
comment: far-call wrapper: xor ax,ax; lcall 0x299:0xdeb; xor ax,ax. Thin wrapper invoking the 0x299:0xdeb far routine with a zero argument

name: seg_setup_clear_ax
comment: segment setup: ds=es=gs; xor ax,ax. Rebases DS/ES to the work arena and zeroes ax before a data operation

name: flag_test_a2e
comment: flag test: ax=[0xa2e]; test al,1; branch. Reads the 0xa2e state word and dispatches on its low bit

name: rtc_time_read
comment: RTC time read: ah=2; int 1Ah (BIOS get real-time-clock time); al=ch; call 0x986. Reads the hardware clock (used to seed timing/PRNG)

name: mem_alloc_64k
comment: memory alloc 64K: ax=8; ebp=0x10000 (65536 bytes); lcall 0x4b9:0 (allocator); gs:[0xa98]=ds (store returned segment). Allocates a 64KB arena and records its segment

name: abs_negate_gs_setup
comment: abs/negate helper: bp=gs; ds=es=gs; si=dx; neg ax. Rebases segments to the work arena and negates/absolutes the ax argument (called 5x)

name: render_state_5251_access
comment: render-state access: bp=di; es->fs; di=0x5251 (the 576-byte render-state block cleared by clear_block_5251 0x248b); push gs. Reads/updates the render state block

name: cs_table_read_1c6
comment: cs-table read: ds=cs; si=0x1c6; si+=0xb; cx=0xa (10 entries). Reads a 10-entry constant table at cs:0x1c6+0xb (sibling of cs_data_ptr_setup 0x24eb)

name: ascii_digit_parse
comment: ASCII digit parse: bl=[si]; cmp bl,0x39 ('9'); jg. Parses a numeric string, testing each byte against the '0'..'9' range

name: kbd_read_int16
comment: keyboard read: ax=0x100; int 16h (AH=01 check-keystroke); if none skip; else xor ax,ax; int 16h (AH=00 get-keystroke). BIOS keyboard poll+read

name: resource_file_load
comment: resource file load: dx=si; call 0x2693 (path build); bp=gs:[0xa8e] (FindFirst file size); cx=bp. Loads a resource file by name - path build then sized read (called 7x, a core loader)

name: bridge_panorama_frame_unpack
comment: (= far 0x1CE:0x0A70) TB.BIG panorama-frame RLE unpacker, NOT a clear (old label wrong): les di,gs:[0x5229]; ebp=0xfa00 = decodes EXACTLY 64000 px (full 320x200) from ds:si. Signed control byte: <0 -> run of (-ctrl+1) copies of next byte, >=0 -> (ctrl+1) literals. gs:[0x5b57]&1 selects TRANSPARENT variant (value 0 skips: window starfield/prev frame shows through) vs OPAQUE. Ported+verified: src/tbbig.rs

name: cmos_rtc_read
comment: CMOS RTC read: xor ax,ax; out 0x70,al (select CMOS register 0); in al,0x71 (read); cs:[0xaee]=ax. Reads the CMOS real-time-clock seconds register directly

name: vga_palette_write
comment: VGA palette (DAC) write: dx=0x3c8; out dx,al (PEL write index); inc dl (0x3c9 = PEL data); cx=0x300 (768 = 256 colors * 3). Uploads the full 256-entry VGA palette to the DAC

name: resource_name_write_c00
comment: resource name-table write: ds=es=fs; [0xc00]=ax; bx=ax. Writes into the FS:0xc00 resource descriptor area (adjacent to the name table fs:0xc04; called 8x)

name: flag_gated_render_2793
comment: flag-gated render: ax=[0x2793]; test al,1; if clear skip to 0x78c9, else es=gs and render. Gates a render pass on the 0x2793 state bit

name: render_dispatch_6788
comment: render dispatch: [0xdb8]=0; ax=[0x6788]; if negative skip; test [0x1fb2],1. Core per-frame render dispatch keyed on the 0x6788 object/scene index (called 8x)

name: audio_param_init_cd5
comment: audio param init: ds=gs; di=0xcd5; cx=9; [di]=ax fill. Initialises the 9-word audio parameter block at gs:0xcd5 (adjacent to the driver callback ptr gs:0xcdf)

name: install_timer_isr_hook
comment: timer ISR install: get INT 08h vector (int 21h AX=3508) -> save original to gs:[0xb1d]/[0xb1f]; then set INT 08h (int 21h AH=25, DS:DX=cs:0x213) to the game's own timer handler. Hooks the PIT timer, chaining to the saved vector

name: timer_isr_handler
comment: game timer ISR (installed at cs:0x213 by install_timer_isr_hook 0x79c; file 0x600+0x213). Per-tick handler that chains to the saved original vector via call gs:[0xb1d]

name: xms_driver_entry_off
comment: XMS driver entry-point OFFSET, obtained at boot via int 2Fh AX=4310 (after the AX=4300 install check). call gs:[0xa4a] (~18 sites) invokes HIMEM.SYS - an EXTERNAL driver, not an internal function

name: xms_driver_entry_seg
comment: XMS driver entry-point SEGMENT (paired with gs:0xa4a). Together the far pointer to the external XMS driver

name: saved_timer_int08_off
comment: saved original INT 08h (PIT timer) vector OFFSET, captured by install_timer_isr_hook (int 21h 3508). The game's timer ISR chains to it via call gs:[0xb1d] - EXTERNAL (BIOS/DOS handler)

name: saved_timer_int08_seg
comment: saved original INT 08h vector SEGMENT (paired with gs:0xb1d)

name: input_dispatch
comment: input command dispatch: lcall 0x1ce:0x39d (read input event -> ax); if AL==0 use AH; or al,0x80 for the alt path; bx=0x113e; xlatb (translate the code via DS:0x113e); if result negative (0x80 bit) ignore; else index*2 and call cs:[bx+0x123e] (handler table at file 0x183e). The central keyboard/command handler dispatcher

name: input_handler_table
comment: input dispatch handler table (cs:0x123e): array of 16-bit cs-relative handler offsets, indexed by the xlatb-translated input code. Entry 0 -> file 0x1f06. Consumed by input_dispatch 0x210e

name: input_xlat_table_ref
comment: input translate table = CS:0x113e (file 0x173e, 237/256 nonzero); handler table = CS:0x123e (file 0x183e). CONFIRMED DYNAMICALLY: a live ptrace read of the running game showed DGROUP-DS:0x113e holds the 'SCRIPT5.VAR' filename buffer (not a table) and DGROUP:0x123e is zero, ruling out the DGROUP base and proving DS=CS at the 0x210e dispatch. The 256-byte xlat table maps each input byte -> handler index (<0x80) or ignore (>=0x80); 51 distinct live indices select word entries in the CS:0x123e handler table

name: game_palette_dac_buffer
comment: 768-byte VGA DAC palette buffer (256 RGB triples, each channel 0-63). Source for vga_palette_write 0x2f90 (rep outsb to port 0x3c9). Uploaded by the palette-upload wrapper at 0x16a7 (ds=gs; si=0x5b58; lcall 0x299:0 -> 0x2f90). = DGROUP:0x5b58 (file 0x12f78 holds the baked default). VERIFIED live via ptrace: 768/768 bytes in valid DAC range. This is the GAME-screen palette (locations/nav/dialogue); the intro HNM videos set the DAC via their own per-frame palette path

name: rle_composite_linear_5229
comment: full-screen RLE compositor into the LINEAR buffer gs:0x5229 - which is a persistent STARFIELD TEXTURE source (NOT the display frame): les di,gs:[0x5229]; ebp=0xfa00 (64000 px); loop reads RLE run bytes from ds:si - negative byte = transparent skip (neg;inc -> cx; add di,cx), positive = opaque run (inc -> cx; rep stosb es:[di],color). PROVES gs:0x5229 is a LINEAR 320x200 row-major back-buffer (di advances 1 byte/pixel, no plane/stride math). Gated on gs:0x5b57 bit0

name: credit_presenter_b_cryo
comment: CRYO credit presenter: copies SI->gs:0xe18, sets 5e64=1 5e58=0 (arms clean reveal). NEVER dispatched (linear 0x8a32 confirmed via exec_watch_linear)

name: credit_presenter_a_waitcmd
comment: WAIT COMMANDER default presenter (cs 0cbd:066e): mov si,0x190; mov di,0xe18; sets 5e58=0xe18+0x12=0xe2a; copies WAIT COMMANDER. RUNS instead of (b)

name: bridge_frame_angle_bias
comment: = bridge_panorama_frame_index*8 - 0xA0, computed at 0x97ed; subtracted from [0xa2a] then wrapped into 0..0x5a0 (1440 units/revolution, 8 units per panorama frame)

name: bridge_station_table
comment: 4 records x 0x18 stride: per-station state; +0x0C..0x13 = the current frame's animated-region bbox {w,h,x,y} (all reset to -1 then the loaded chunk's bbox copied into the entry its word@+8 selects). Stations: 0=wide helm (frames 0-21+160-179), 1=golden console menu (22-71, rest=55), 2=pyramid nav room (72-107), 3=organic Orxx (108-159)

name: panorama_transparent_flag
comment: bit0: bridge_panorama_frame_unpack variant select - 1=transparent (value 0 preserves underlying pixel; set at 0x958c rotate path), 0=opaque full redraw (0x95c4 station-entry path)

name: panorama_palette_refresh_flag
comment: bit0: after a panorama frame unpack, copy master palette 0x5b58 -> 0x5251 (0x9893)

name: bridge_frame_to_yaw_sync
comment: (retf entry) copies bridge_panorama_frame_index [0x2795] -> ship-3D yaw index [0x2f6d]; computes [0x27a7]=frame*8-0xa0; rewraps [0xa2a] relative to it into 0..0x5a0. Ties the 180 panorama frames 1:1 to the 180x2deg ship-3D angle table

name: tbbig_open
comment: boot-time open of tb.big: dx=0xD3 (filename in DS), path-build lcall 0x1ce:0x3b3, int21 ax=3D00, handle -> [0xac4]

name: bridge_steer_update
comment: per-tick bridge view update: if seek ([0x2793]&8) ease frame toward [0x279b]/2 by half-remaining (min 1) shortest way on the 180 ring, long seeks ([0x279d]>=0x28) drag the cursor ring anchor along; else mouse-push steering: dist(frame*2, [0xa2a]/4) on the 360 arc ring, dead zone <=0x1f, view lands 0x1e arc short of the cursor ([0x97c4]/[0x97d4]); [0x2793]&4 = menu-engaged clamp mode (drags cursor back at 0x28). Falls into 0x97e4 sync + 0x97fc screen rebase. Ported: src/bridge.rs BridgeView::update_view (replays all 3 BRIDGEPROBE observations)

name: console_menu_hit_test
comment: golden-menu hit+highlight (gated bridge frame 0x28..0x3c): box right=0x11f-delta*8, width 0x6e (177..287 at rest frame 45); rows top=0x48+|delta|*1.25 pitch 0x12-|delta|/8, 5 rows; hover row DAC 0x7b+row <- (63,0,0) red, idle 5 rows <- (16,12,0) dark gold; click: [0x2a19]=row+1, [0x2793]|=0xc, seek [0x279b]=0x5a (centre menu), highlight y -> [0x253f], UI sound ax=4 lcall 0xb1b:0x11d. Ported: src/bridge.rs menu_row_under_cursor/click/apply_menu_palette

name: console_menu_selected_item
comment: selected golden-menu row+1 (1..5 = HONK/TELEPHONE/CRYOBOX/MENU/OPTION; 0=none). Set by 0x8613 click path; ored into the bridge busy gate at 0x7d96 (station orb clicks disabled while engaged)

name: bridge_seek_target_arc
comment: station-seek target in arc units (= 2*frame). 0x5a=menu(45); station records [0x2a1b+rec*0x18+0xa] hold each station's rest target (0/0x5a/0xb4/0x10e = frames 0/45/90/135, live-dumped)

name: bridge_seek_initial_dist
comment: first-tick seek distance memo (frames); >=0x28 gates dragging the cursor ring anchor with the rotation (0x96d0)

name: bridge_turn_direction_flag
comment: 1 = turning one way, 0 = the other (set by both the steer and seek paths)

name: bridge_mouse_y
comment: mouse y (screen space), hit-tested against box y/h at 0x8269/0x827f

name: mouse_button_down_flag
comment: bit0 gates the box hit tests 0x8269/0x8295 (only while the button is down)

name: mouse_hit_test
comment: box hit test: si -> {x,y,w,h} 8-byte rect; inside = [0xa2a]>=x && [0xa2a]-w<=x && [0xa2c]>=y && [0xa2c]-h<=y; on hit or byte [bp],8. PROVES the station/orb rect field order {x,y,w,h} (tbbig chunk header + station table +0xc)

name: entity_object_populate
comment: ACTIVATES an entity: AX=id DX=resource handle -> di=0x6212+id*32; resolve handle 0x4b9:0x190; flags=([res_hdr]&4)|0x83 (0x80 active bit) at +0; unpack object far ptr +4/+6; copy data +0xc/+0xe; init +0x14/+0x16. THE entity-activation primitive (nav destinations 0x15..0x1F, crew, location content). = 0x299:0x1140

name: object_population_loop
comment: after a resource loads, iterates object descriptors at DS:0x2AD3 (di=[bp], stride dx=0x2c), reads kind es:[di]&0x100/0x10 + resource handle es:[di+0x18/0x1a], calls entity_object_populate 0x40D0 for each -> populates+activates a run of entity records. [0x2790]=sub-object count, [0x27C1]=active count. The world-object-instance loader; gate for nav destinations/#3 + location content/#6

name: object_descriptor_table
comment: per-world object descriptor table walked by object_population_loop 0x8D2A (0x2c stride); each entry {kind@0, resource handle@0x18/0x1a} -> entity_object_populate

name: dlg_reveal_pump
comment: subtitle reveal pump: advances gs:0x5e58 one char per gs:0xb31 timer tick; draws via 0x299:0xa2b/0xb23 per line record (bp=0x5e6f/0x5eaf); credit vs reveal path via 0x5e65

name: subtitle_render_string
comment: REAL subtitle glyph blitter: fixed-width console font (ascii map DS:0x70fa=file0x1451A, glyphs DS:0x71aa=file0x145CA); draws to mode-X buffer; color 0xff stepping 0xfe/0xfd at reveal edge. Ported: font::BoldConsoleFont + engine::draw_subtitle_revealed (green fade from baked palette 0xfd/0xfe/0xff)

name: dlg_chatter_burble
comment: talk BURBLE: while [0xcfb] set (line revealing), every 4 ticks ([0xb2f]) play random tb.snd clip 7+rand(0..9), != previous ([0xc4d]). Ported: main.rs chatter loop

name: bridge_steer_ring
comment: bridge view steer tracks mouse in the 1440-px RING ([0xa2a]), not screen — edge-push keeps rotating. Ported: MouseInput.dx/dy raw deltas -> BridgeView

name: zoom_scale_table
comment: 6-step zoom/scale animation (x155..0 y67..10 w10..320), counter [0x2b93] 0..6 — NOT subtitle positions

name: presentation_scan
comment: post-VM record scan: walks the 0x672c directory; ACTIVE records (+2 bit0) by kind: kind 2 = character display maintenance (primary C4 0x675E, wildcard related 0x674E, field ptr follow); kind 1 = PRESENTATION START when the record's C4 field (+0x13 field) is armed: sets 0x67AC=1 active + 0x67B7 start-lock + 0x2793|=4 busy, clears dialogue state (0x6782/0x6784/0x6776/0x67F8/0x67BA..0x67BC/0x679A), record +3 |= 0x80. Kinds 0x10/0x200 -> 0x5A51 shared path. Ported effect: VmMachine::start_actor_presentation + the engine dialogue lifecycle

name: record_c1_ship3d_action
comment: kinds 0x10/0x200 record action from the presentation scan: acts when the record field is typed 0xC1 (the ship-3D opcode) — nav/ship-3D presentation maintenance, gated on gs:0x6752/0x27DF. NOT a Sequence-cutscene dispatch

name: manu3_api_entry
comment: manu3.xdb far-call API: [bp]=cursor x/y dwords -> [0x1a]/[0x1c]; [bp+4]&0x1f = function selector (call 0x181 if set); [bp+6]>>4+0xA000 -> [0x18] caller seg. CURSOR->POSE: yaw [0x23e4] += (x-160)*2, pitch [0x23e2] += (y-100)*2 around the render call 0x270, then restored

name: manu3_matrix_build
comment: builds the Q15 rotation matrix at 0x2250..0x226F from THREE Euler angles at state+0x4E/0x50/0x52 (state block 0x2336, active record +0x5E; angles masked 0xFFC = dword offsets into the sin/cos tables, pairs at [tbl+0x26]/[tbl+0x28]); combines via angle-sum identities (add/sub + sar 1)

name: manu3_transform
comment: VERIFIED: Q15 3x3 transform, matrix 0x2250, dword vertex triples [si] -> [di], translation [si+0x36]; then walks a vertex CHAIN (mov si,[di]; lea si/di,+0x12) — 3 links per record (the skeletal chain)

name: manu3_texture_data
comment: texture pixel runs (teal ramp 0xF0-0xF9 + 0xD5/0xDD family) — the hand's affine texture source

name: manu3_entity_project
comment: second API entry: projects PARENT-segment objects (es=[2]; i16 vectors at es:[si+4/6/8], depth out es:[si+0xE], flag es:[si+0x12]=0x8000) through the state record's 3x4 matrix (+0x12..+0x35 rows, +0x36/3A/3E translation, sar 8) — the same structure as the main EXE's 0x9B98 nav projection. State record: +2=count->0x224A, +6=object ptr

name: manu3_face_bucket_sort
comment: triangle setup: face records 8 bytes {next, v0ptr, v1ptr, v2ptr} from fs:[0x2300] (count fs:[0x2304]) — a per-frame WORK buffer @0xF137 (zeroed in file); clip = AND of vertex +0x12 flags; Y-sort the 3 verts (screen Y at vtx+0x0A); insert into the Y-BUCKET chain table @0x686 (2 bytes/scanline) for scanline rendering. SOURCE mesh + face builder = the stage between the transform loop and 0x700 (next trace)

name: manu3_vertex_stride
comment: projected-vertex records stride 0x14 (20B); per-object vertex count [0x224A] (rec+2), object count [0x22F2]; 0x68A = alias-vertex copy pass ([0x22FA] list, +4 = source-vertex ptr — mirrored/shared verts copy screen results)

name: manu3_state_runtime
comment: state block is RUNTIME-BUILT (file image holds garbage counts/ptrs): the INIT function (selector path via 0x181/0x19B) unpacks the model records — trace init to find the packed hand mesh in the xdb

name: manu3_anim_select
comment: function selector -> relative-offset SEQUENCE table at [0x2306] (=0x3E72): picks an animation/script sequence ([0x102E] cursor, 0x1032 active-tween list)

name: manu3_tween_step
comment: per-frame tween processor: records {counter[di], target[di+4], value[di+8], accum dword [di+6] += step [di+0xA]} -> writes value to *target; expired tweens swap-removed. The hand poses + menu motions are DATA-DRIVEN tweens; targets ~0x2670-0x2870 region

name: manu3_span_renderer_init
comment: scanline S-BUFFER renderer: free-list of 200 edge/span records (stride 0x5A from 0xA72), sentinel spans at 0x964/0x9BE/0xA18 with +/-0x7FFFFFFF depth sentinels, active-table head [0xA28]; scans the Y-bucket table for non-empty scanlines (repe scasw). Faces insert depth-sorted spans per scanline; the affine fill textures each visible span — classic Cryo span engine

name: manu3_data_map
comment: 16 paired pointers {runtime work buffer 0xEFB1..0xF573, packed SOURCE 0x3DFC..0x3F30} — the xdb's complete data map (faces pair = [0x2300]work/[0x2302]src=0x3E5C). Source blocks are copy DESCRIPTORS ({addr,len,stride,addr}, face stride 9 = packed 9-byte face records); exact src/dst semantics need the init copier that consumes the descriptor pairs (next trace)

name: manu3_data_map_correction
comment: CORRECTION: code reads [0x22F2]=object COUNT, [0x22FA]=alias ptr, [0x22FE]=alias count, [0x2300]=face ptr, [0x2304]=face count, [0x2306]=seq table — but the FILE values are implausible as counts => an INIT (EXE-side loader patch or in-xdb init) rewrites this block after load. fs:[2]/fs:[6] = parent/screen segments from a PATCHED header area. Next: trace the EXE's xdb load+first-call protocol (resource_load_by_id 0x287B onward) for the patch/init step

name: manu3_init_protocol
comment: croolis's decoded overlay protocol generalizes: overlays self-relocate (segment table at file 0x0C..0x10) and their INIT fills the fs:0x22F0 pointer block with load-segment-relocated values (croolis: fs:0x22F0=0xFF11, 0x22F4=0xD9C2...). manu3's stale file values = build-time layout; init rewrites. Packed sources = the 0x3DFC.. descriptor blocks; the init body (head code past the 0x121 skip target) is the next disassembly slice to settle descriptor {src,len,stride,dst} semantics

name: manu3_reloc_key
comment: cs:[0x1368] = data-seg delta (0x137 paras): ds:0 = file+0x1370. Derived segs [2]/[4]/[6] = ds + file[0xC/0xE/0x10]. Entry 0x150 = per-frame no-cursor variant (tween->matrix->project->0x6F6 face-build+render); 0x6F6 = the face builder. Sane post-reloc reads: 16 objects, seq table ds:0x2974; face ptr/count cells ds:0x2300/0x2304 are STILL runtime-filled (stale in file) => definitive mesh read = LIVE memory dump of the resident xdb segment from the savestate (XDBDUMP probe, next slice)

name: manu3_segments_resolved
comment: RESOLVED: the old notes' 'ds 0x17A3/0x1C94/0x2094' are SEGMENTS — 0x166C code, 0x17A3 data (ds), 0x1B76/0x1C94/0x2094 derived work segs (ds:[2]/[4]/[6]). Live dumps in accuracy/manu3/ (ds + all three derived): 216 faces at ds:0xB18 ({next,v0,v1,v2} ptrs), vertex records in the work segs (source coords via es=[2] in projection). Mesh extraction now offline-analyzable

name: manu3_face_builder_next
comment: ACTIVE-RENDER dumps banked (accuracy/manu3/, mouse-driven state; span free-list churned = renderer ran; face links converged steady-state). Face-record pointer decode needs 0x6F6 (the face builder) disassembled to settle which of the 216 records are live and the vertex-record segment/stride (seg2 candidate: rec ~{...,x,y,z,...} i16; pointers overlap => sub-lists per the 0x30/0x88/0x27C descriptor groups). NEXT SLICE: disassemble 0x6F6..0x700

name: resource_subobject_dir
comment: resource sub-object directory decode (inside entity_object_populate): resource header {flags:u16, count:u16}, then count dword entries at +4 — entry nibble0 = byte offset, entry>>4 = PARAGRAPH offset from the resource ds; sub-object far ptr = (ds+paras):offset. Entity id -> sub-object index. The .ext/.spr resources are sub-object directories; the world/entity click path goes through the C1/ship-3D records (0x5B38 family) — the converged cyberspace/on-planet trace continues there

name: c1_present_select
comment: C1 action body: field 0x11 = presented-object link; kind 0x10 vs 0x200 paths; for 0x200 (ship active [0x24F3]&1) compares DI vs gs:[0x251B] = the CURRENTLY-PRESENTED world object — differs -> new presentation setup (es=[0x6726]); same -> 0x5C26 teardown path. gs:[0x251B] is the ship-3D selection cell the mouse hit-test upstream writes (ship FSM 0xAFA0 -> entity rect test) — the remaining link in the world-click chain

name: ship_click_commit
comment: ship-3D click commit: [0x251B] = selected entity record (from es:[di+0x16] / hover cell [0x250B], minus 4); sets FSM [0x6788]=3 (PRESENT-OBJECT state), [0x252D]/[0x252E]=1, resets text state, then display copy + plane band. The world-click chain: hover cell [0x250B] (hit-test upstream, next slice) -> commit here -> C1 select (0x5B75) -> script blocks

name: entity_candidate_list
comment: builds the PRESENTABLE-OBJECT candidate list: source list @gs:0x6886 (0x624B nav-source build), filtered by flags&0x98 + rec[+2]&2, survivors written to [0x250B]++ — the world/entity selection is LIST-DRIVEN (choice-box rows = candidates; commit picks via +0x16 linkage). NO free-roam mouse hover hit-test exists — consistent with the universal choice-box console model. Port adjustment: on-planet interaction = entity list box, not free clicks

name: resource_class_taxonomy
comment: resource header CONFIRMED on real files: flags=4 = SPRITE BANK sub-object directory (CARTE.SPR {4,7}, BTV.SPR {4,11} — all pointers valid); flags=2 = WORLD container (.ext) with its OWN body format (the 3-byte node table framing, not the dword dir). Sub-object enumeration of every .SPR bank is now mechanical

name: manu3_affine_fill
comment: THE textured inner loop (column-oriented): per row {u(ax) += du(bp); v(dx) += dv(si); texel = ds:[bh:bl] = tex[v.hi*256+u.hi] (256x256 texture, ds = texture seg from lds [rec+0x54]); es:[di]=texel; di += 0x140 (next row)}. Span/edge record fields: +2 flags (0x8001 end/skip), +4/+6 links, +0xA coord, +0x42 u, +0x44 v, +0x52 du, +0x54 dv + texture far ptr. Column S-buffer fill. With this, EVERY manu3 mechanism is decoded — the Rust transcription is fully specified

name: manu3_span_setup_next
comment: NEGATIVE banked: vertex +0xA/+0xC are PROJECTED coords (u 212..294 = screen-ish), not UVs — per-vertex UVs falsified. The u/v gradient source must come from the span-setup region (0x848..0xBE6: face -> span records with +0x42 u/+0x44 v/+0x52 du/+0x54 dv+texseg) — that disassembly is the single remaining decode for textured transcription

name: manu3_span_insert
comment: depth-sorted span insert: spans linked by {next[0], prev[0x10]}, ordered by depth dword +8 (sentinels 0x964/0xA18); column cursor [0x680] 0..0x140 walks columns; [0x684] = per-column face-bucket cursor; faces pop at 0x8B1 -> triangle->edge/gradient conversion 0x8B1..0xBE6 (the u/v gradient math, next slices)

name: manu3_face_activate
comment: per-face activation (called per bucket face from 0x8C6): converts a face into EDGE records with linear interpolators {value = base(+0x20) + coord(+0xA)*step(+0x28)} — the u/v/depth gradient setup lives here (next slice). Edge flags +0x1A bit15; per-edge eval at 0x90C (eax = -coord*step+base)

name: manu3_gradient_setup
comment: triangle gradient setup: loads the 3 verts' packed screen dwords (+0xA = x:y), deltas -> RECIPROCAL TABLE lookups ([delta*4], the div-free gradient trick), cross-product area with sign backface-reject (jge 0x118E), sar 8 scale; degenerate-edge specials at 0x118F/0x1366; writes interpolator cells {+0xC, +0x1C, +0x2E count}. Continue transcription from here (~1.5KB routine)

name: manu3_vertex_source_model
comment: INSIGHT (explains the degenerate render): the hand is SKELETAL — the transform walks CHAINED state records (dword position vectors at +0x12.., translations +0x36) = the skeleton segments; the 20B pool's +4..+8 i16 are vertex offsets RELATIVE to their segment (z-hugging values = fingers along the segment axis). Faithful assembly = per-segment matrix+translation THEN the relative offsets. Next: extract the segment chain (positions/parents) from the live ds state records and compose

name: manu3_segment_records
comment: live segment records: seg0 {cnt=14, T=(1024,-3456,16000)>>8, ang≈rest} and seg1 {cnt=16} sane at stride 0x60 — but records 2+ read garbage at that stride => the object-loop's record ADVANCE (who updates [0x2248] between objects, around the 0x544 'mov di,[0x2248]; jne 0x279' loop) is the pinned next decode for the true stride/layout

name: manu3_pose_sequences
comment: the REAL sequence table (post-reloc): 8 pose-animation sequences; records target the SEGMENT ANGLE CELLS (0x23E2/0x23E4 wrist; +0x5E steps: 0x2440 seg1, 0x249E seg2, 0x24FC seg3...) with step values — the function selectors = named hand poses (point/grab/...) tween-animating the fingers. Extractable for the pose-refinement work (record family {counter/flags, target, value, step} per the 0x19B processor)

name: manu3_pose_seq0
comment: pose sequence 0 stream: 4-word groups {delta, count, cellA, cellB} walking the segment angle cells (0x23E2,0x23E4,0x23E6.. and 0x27EC/0x28AA-family = the T cells!) — keyframes drive BOTH angles and translations. The 0x181 setup builds active-tween records {counter,target,value,step} from these groups; exact word roles resolve against that constructor when the pose extraction is implemented

name: manu3_tween_constructor
comment: EXACT tween-group spec: 8-byte groups {word0: count.lo/phase.hi, word1: (unused/flags), word2: target cell, word3: END VALUE}; construction: step=(end - *target)<<16 / count (Q16/frame), accum=(*target<<16)+step, count-1 -> active record {counter, target, accum, step}; groups gate on phase == [0x102C] (sequences are PHASED, constructor advances the phase). Pose-player implementation fully specified

name: manu3_load_site
comment: manu3.xdb load-by-name (si=DS:0x113 'manu3.xdb' -> lcall 0x1CE:0x7DB loader) inside a restore path (after the 3D.snd presentation). The per-frame CALL site with the pose selector = the consumer of the loader's stored segment (loader-return storage -> next slice)

name: alien_overlay_cycle
comment: the {amer, croolis, scrut}.xdb overlay CYCLE (name table DS:0xACC, index [0xAE5] 0..2) with the cursor pushed around the load/call — the ALIEN-EXAMINATION screen path (validates the port's alien_view cycling). NOT the hand's per-frame caller (still pinned: the manu3 segment-storage consumer)

name: xms_driver_entry
comment: XMS driver far entry (int 2F ax=4310 -> bx:es stored here); the many lcall [0xA4A] sites = XMS calls (NOT overlay dispatch). Remaining overlay far-pointer candidates for the manu3 per-frame caller: [0xCD3]/[0xCDB]/[0xCDF]/[0xCEB]/[0xCF3] (next slice)

name: driver_entry_table
comment: [0xCD3]/[0xCDB]/[0xCDF]/[0xCEB]/[0xCF3] = far pointers into the loaded DRIVER (dnsdb/nosound.drv) at entries 0x100/0x106/0x109/0x112/0x118 (segment word patched at load) — the sound-driver dispatch, NOT manu3. Manu3's per-frame caller: likely a RELOC'D direct far call (fixed deterministic load segment) — search the reloc table for calls into the manu3 segment range (pinned)

name: manu3_perframe_caller
comment: CALLERWATCH result: manu3 entry 0x0000 is far-called from 0x022D:0x07F2 (return addr; call inside gfx_clipped_primitive_a 0x32AC) — the hand draw is dispatched from the GRAPHICS-PRIMITIVE layer during frame composition; the pose selector ([bp+4]) = this primitive's parameter from ITS caller (next hop). Secondary entry ip=0x886 called from 0x0E84:0x011F (the gs-segment trampoline)

name: hand_call_protocol
comment: THE COMPLETE per-frame hand call (file 0x167D..0x16A2): argument frame @DS:0xAB4 = {cursor_x [0xA2A], cursor_y [0xA2C], SELECTOR [0xA32], target seg [0x5219]}; lcall [0xA96] (the loaded-overlay far-pointer slot); [0xAE7] = hand-hide countdown (skip frames). SELECTOR VALUES from writers: 1 @0x58FD (presentation start), 0xB @0x154B (UI close), 0xFFFF @0x18F9 (none/hide); full map = the remaining writer sweep. The pose-per-context system is now protocol-complete

name: hand_pose_selector_map
comment: FULL immediate-write map: 29 sites, selectors 0..0x10 — 1 @0x14D8/0x58FE/0x59A3/0x7809/0x8548/0x8A57/0x8B8D/0xB0BC (presentation/default point), 2 @0x781E, 3 @0x782C, 4 @0x8227, 5 @0x86AB, 6 @0x851C, 7 @0x8529, 9 @0x7911, 0xA @0x7FBF/0x80C1, 0xB @0x1549/0x7EE3/0x8FFA (UI close), 0xC @0x7962, 0xD @0x8190, 0xE @0x7BEA, 0x10 @0x814D, 0 @0x1649/0x1980/0x8B3B, 0xFFFF @0x18F9/0x8AE4 (hidden). The 0x78xx-0x8Bxx cluster = the console-interaction handlers: each site's context = its screen state (readable on demand). Pose system fully enumerated

name: selector_contexts_2
comment: more contexts: 4 @0x8227 = presentation-record active ([0x676A]/[0x675A]) — the attentive/talking pose; 6/7 @0x851C/0x8529 = CHOICE-BOX hover poses (the row math div-0xB / [0x27C7]=row+1 sits at 0x8500); the 0x8690 DAC writes = the selection glow. Sweep continues per-site as needed

name: ui_region_table_scan
comment: the console's clickable-REGION scanner (returns a region id; 0x1F = the region selecting pose 0xC) — the hit-region table system behind the pose selectors and click dispatch. Selector contexts: 9 @0x7911 = region-enter edge ([0x27EA]); 0xC @0x7962 = region 0x1F hover; 0xE @0x7BEA = post-0x971-call state. Remaining sites sweep mechanically against this scanner's region ids

name: ui_region_table
comment: the console's 32 x 32B ui-region table (descending from ds:0x65F2), RUNTIME-populated: at the pinned hub only region 4 is active (flag 0x84) — regions activate per console state. Record format = whatever 0x8295 (flag_gated_compare_a3e) tests vs the cursor cells [0xA3E] (pinned next). REGIONDUMP probe added

name: region_record_hittest
comment: DECODED: region record = {flag@+0, ..., rect@+8: x,y,w,h i16} in RING-space cursor coords ([0xA2A]/[0xA2C]); hit = x>=rx && x<=rx+w && y>=ry && y<=ry+h, gated on mouse-enable [0xA3E]&1. The console hit-region system is fully decoded (scanner 0x82C3 + record format + coordinate space) — port hit-tests validatable against live per-state tables via REGIONDUMP

name: region4_orb_validated
comment: LIVE VALIDATION: during the pinned presentation exactly ONE region is active — region 4, rect (137,139 50x44) = THE ORB's hit box (click-to-advance) — confirming the port's model (presenting: only advance-clicks; idle: full dispatch). The region table is the ground truth for every console hit-test per state

name: region_table_scope
comment: UNPIN dump: the region table holds ONLY the orb zone even on the unpinned hub — the golden-menu rows use the OTHER hit system (the STATION RECORDS, 0x7DAE cs:0x06D4 dispatch by record kind). TWO hit systems total: region table (orb/zones) + station records (menu/stations) — both now structurally documented

name: station_record_dispatch
comment: station record format: {+0 flags (bit0 active, bit2 mouse-disable clears [0xA3E]/[0xA40], bit3 auto-seek), +0xA target ARC (-> [0x279B] seek target when bit3 and view not there = the auto-seek-to-station mechanism), +0xC.. hit rect -> mouse_hit_test 0x8269}. Validates the port's BridgeView station records + seek behavior
